AA.description: Insertion/Insertion genotype, lowest ACE levels, endurance advantage, lower blood pressure

AG.description: Insertion/Deletion genotype, intermediate ACE levels and athletic profile

GG.description: Deletion/Deletion genotype, highest ACE levels, power/strength advantage, higher blood pressure tendency

description: ACE insertion/deletion polymorphism is the primary determinant of circulating ACE levels. The D allele doubles ACE activity, affecting blood pressure, athletic performance, and cardiac remodeling.
notes: This is a proxy SNP for the classic ACE I/D (287bp Alu insertion/deletion). II genotype prevalent in endurance athletes, DD in power athletes. D allele also associated with hypertension and left ventricular hypertrophy.

CC.description: Lower tendon injury risk

CT.description: Average injury risk

TT.description: Higher Achilles tendon injury susceptibility

description: Collagen type V variant affecting tendon flexibility and injury predisposition.

sources.name: Mokone GG et al. (2006) - The COL5A1 gene and Achilles tendon pathology

notes: COL5A1 variant associated with Achilles tendon injury risk in athletes. Effect has been replicated in multiple sports medicine studies. Risk is modifiable through training load management and proper conditioning. Most relevant for competitive athletes.

GG.description: Homozygous reference (Arg16); standard beta-2 adrenergic receptor expression and bronchodilatory response. Arg16 receptors show higher agonist-induced downregulation compared to Gly16.

GA.description: Heterozygous Arg16/Gly16; intermediate receptor downregulation profile. Response to beta-agonists (exercise-induced epinephrine, bronchodilator medications) is between the two homozygous phenotypes.

AA.description: Homozygous alternate (Gly16); Gly16 receptors show attenuated agonist-induced downregulation, maintaining higher surface receptor density during sustained adrenergic stimulation. Associated with improved endurance performance in some athlete studies.

description: ADRB2 encodes the beta-2 adrenergic receptor, which mediates sympathetic nervous system effects on bronchial smooth muscle, skeletal muscle vasodilation, and lipolysis. The rs1042713 variant causes an Arg16Gly amino acid substitution that alters receptor downregulation kinetics in response to sustained agonist stimulation. The Gly16 (A allele) form resists downregulation better, potentially maintaining higher adrenergic responsiveness during prolonged exercise. Some studies associate Gly16 with superior endurance capacity, though effect sizes are modest.

sources.name: Large V et al. (1997) - Human beta2-adrenoceptor gene polymorphisms and obesity in French subjects

notes: The A (Gly16) allele frequency is ~37-53% globally with modest population variation (East Asian ~53%, European ~37%). The fitness association is most relevant for endurance athletes and response to beta-agonist bronchodilators (asthma medications). Clinically, this variant is also studied in asthma pharmacogenomics — Gly16 homozygotes may show different long-acting beta-agonist response. This is a risk modifier, not a diagnosis. Discuss with a healthcare provider if using beta-agonist medications.

AA.description: Homozygous reference; typical beta-2 adrenergic bronchodilation during exercise, standard airway conductance under sympathetic stimulation.

AG.description: Heterozygous; moderate variation in beta-2 adrenergic receptor kinetics affecting exercise-induced bronchodilation.

GG.description: Homozygous alternate; altered receptor regulation associated with enhanced beta-2 adrenergic airway response and better oxygen delivery during sustained aerobic effort.

description: The ADRB2 gene encodes the beta-2 adrenergic receptor, a key mediator of sympathetic bronchodilation in airway smooth muscle. The rs7460 variant affects receptor trafficking and downregulation kinetics, influencing the degree of exercise-induced bronchodilation. Superior airway dilation during endurance exercise improves ventilatory efficiency and oxygen delivery.

sources.name: Gougat C et al. (2000) - ADRB2 polymorphisms and respiratory phenotypes

notes: ADRB2 variants are particularly relevant for athletes with exercise-induced bronchoconstriction or asthma. The rs7460 variant is in partial LD with the well-studied Arg16Gly (rs1042713) and Gln27Glu (rs1042714) variants. Most athletic association studies used haplotype-level analysis.

GG.description: Homozygous reference; typical PPARα-mediated fatty acid oxidation gene regulation, standard aerobic adaptation to endurance training.

GC.description: Heterozygous; intermediate PPARα activity with somewhat enhanced mitochondrial biogenesis response to endurance training.

CC.description: Homozygous alternate; higher PPARα transcriptional activity promotes greater mitochondrial density and fatty acid oxidation capacity in response to aerobic training.

description: PPARA encodes peroxisome proliferator-activated receptor alpha, a nuclear receptor that regulates fatty acid oxidation and mitochondrial biogenesis genes. The rs4253778 G/C variant influences PPARα expression and transcriptional activity, modulating the aerobic metabolic adaptations that drive VO2max improvements with endurance training.

sources.name: Vohl MC et al. (2004) - A gene for human skeletal-muscle lipoprotein lipase

notes: PPARα variants are most predictive of endurance training response when combined with training volume data. C allele frequency is approximately 15-25% in European populations. Effect on absolute VO2max is modest and training-dependent.

AA.description: Lower ACE activity, improved endurance performance, efficient oxygen utilization

AG.description: Intermediate ACE levels, mixed endurance and power capacity

GG.description: Higher ACE activity, increased angiotensin II, favors power and strength performance

description: ACE insertion/deletion polymorphism affects circulating and tissue ACE levels. The I allele (lower ACE) favors endurance, while the D allele (higher ACE) favors power and strength.
notes: ACE I/D is one of the most studied sports genetics variants. D allele frequency ~55% globally. Effect sizes are modest but consistent across studies. Also relevant to hypertension risk and ACE inhibitor response.

CC.description: Reduced IL-6 production, potentially slower inflammatory signaling and exercise recovery

CG.description: Moderate IL-6 production, balanced inflammatory and recovery response

GG.description: Higher IL-6 production, stronger exercise-induced inflammatory response, may recover faster

description: IL-6 -174G>C promoter variant affects interleukin-6 expression, a key myokine released during exercise that mediates training adaptations, glucose uptake, and recovery signaling.
notes: IL-6 has dual roles — pro-inflammatory systemically but anti-inflammatory as a myokine during exercise. The G allele increases IL-6 transcription. Associated with exercise recovery, body composition, and metabolic health.

CC.description: Higher PPARdelta activity, improved fat burning during exercise, better endurance capacity

CT.description: Moderate PPARdelta-mediated fat metabolism during exercise

TT.description: Baseline PPARdelta activity, standard fat metabolism during exercise

description: PPARD +294T>C variant increases PPARdelta expression, enhancing fatty acid oxidation in skeletal muscle. PPARdelta regulates the metabolic switch from glucose to fat burning during exercise.
notes: PPARdelta activation increases slow-twitch muscle fiber proportion and mitochondrial biogenesis. The C allele enhances endurance capacity. PPARdelta agonists (GW501516) are banned performance-enhancing drugs.

AA.description: Homozygous reference; typical HIF-1α transcriptional activity supporting standard hypoxic ventilatory and erythropoietic responses.

AG.description: Heterozygous; modest enhancement of HIF-1α-mediated hypoxia responses, with intermediate erythropoietic and angiogenic adaptation.

GG.description: Homozygous alternate; augmented HIF-1α pathway activity supports stronger erythropoietin induction, higher hemoglobin mass, and better oxygen delivery at altitude.

description: HIF1A encodes hypoxia-inducible factor 1-alpha, the master transcription factor coordinating cellular responses to low oxygen. Under hypoxia HIF-1α activates erythropoietin, VEGF, and glycolytic genes to maintain oxygen delivery. Variants affecting HIF1A expression or stability modulate the magnitude of altitude adaptation and aerobic exercise responses.

sources.name: Prabhakar NR et al. (2014) - Hypoxia-inducible factors and altitude adaptation

notes: High-altitude adaptation involves additional loci (EPAS1, EGLN1) with larger effects in indigenous high-altitude populations. This variant's contribution to acute altitude sickness prevention is modest. Training at altitude remains the primary determinant of altitude performance.

CC.description: Wild-type HFE at H63D position, standard iron absorption

CG.description: HFE H63D carrier, slightly increased iron absorption, usually clinically insignificant alone

GG.description: Homozygous HFE H63D, moderately increased iron absorption, monitor ferritin levels

description: HFE H63D is a common hemochromatosis-associated variant with milder effect than C282Y. Compound heterozygosity (C282Y/H63D) can cause clinical hemochromatosis.
notes: H63D carrier frequency ~15% in Europeans. Alone, rarely causes clinical hemochromatosis. Compound heterozygosity with C282Y (~1% of population) can cause iron overload. Higher iron may benefit athletic performance.

AA.description: Normal MCT1 activity, efficient lactate clearance during high-intensity exercise

AT.description: Moderately reduced MCT1 transporter function, intermediate lactate buffering

TT.description: Reduced MCT1 activity, slower lactate removal, earlier fatigue during intense exercise

description: MCT1 (SLC16A1) A1470T variant affects monocarboxylate transporter 1, the primary lactate shuttle in skeletal muscle. Influences exercise tolerance and recovery between high-intensity efforts.
notes: MCT1 transports lactate across cell membranes for use as fuel by oxidative fibers, heart, and brain. The T allele reduces transport efficiency. Important for repeated sprint and interval training performance.

CC.description: Standard endurance capacity

CT.description: Moderate endurance advantage

TT.description: Enhanced mitochondrial efficiency for endurance

description: Uncoupling protein variant affecting mitochondrial energy efficiency during prolonged exercise.

sources.name: Dhamrait SS et al. (2004) - Variation in the uncoupling protein 2 and 3 genes and their association with type 2 diabetes and physical performance

notes: UCP2 variant studied for associations with endurance performance and metabolic efficiency. Evidence is mixed across studies. Athletic performance is extremely polygenic with major environmental determinants (training, nutrition). No single variant meaningfully predicts athletic potential.

GG.description: Homozygous reference; baseline intronic ACE expression context. The G allele reflects standard intronic architecture near the ACE I/D polymorphism region, with associated standard ACE serum levels and muscle physiology.

GC.description: Heterozygous; intermediate ACE intronic splicing/expression context. Muscle efficiency response under sustained load falls between GG and CC profiles in this additive model.

CC.description: Homozygous alternate; the C allele is at ~45-48% frequency globally. Associated in some studies with reduced ACE activity and better endurance performance efficiency due to altered angiotensin-bradykinin balance in skeletal muscle vasculature.

description: ACE (angiotensin-converting enzyme) converts angiotensin I to angiotensin II and inactivates bradykinin, regulating vasoconstriction, blood pressure, and skeletal muscle oxygen delivery. The rs4341 intronic variant is in high linkage disequilibrium with the classic ACE insertion/deletion (I/D) polymorphism and captures variation in ACE expression levels. Higher ACE activity (D haplotype) is linked to power/strength performance, while lower ACE activity (I haplotype) is associated with endurance efficiency via improved skeletal muscle microcirculation.

sources.name: Myerson S et al. (2001) - Human angiotensin I-converting enzyme gene and endurance performance

notes: The C allele is common globally (~45-48%) with modest population variation (East Asian ~65-68%, African ~42%). The ACE I/D association with athletic performance is one of the most replicated sport genetics findings, though effect sizes are small and no single variant determines athletic outcome. Population studies show East Asians have higher C allele frequencies. This variant captures ACE activity variation related to blood pressure and cardiac remodeling — individuals with ACE inhibitor prescriptions should discuss pharmacogenomic implications with their physician.

CC.description: Homozygous reference; higher NOS3 promoter activity supports greater endothelial nitric oxide synthesis, promoting vasodilation and improved oxygen delivery during exercise.

CT.description: Heterozygous; moderate NOS3 expression with intermediate nitric oxide output and vascular response to exercise.

TT.description: Homozygous alternate; reduced NOS3 promoter activity lowers endothelial NO synthesis, potentially limiting exercise vasodilation and endurance capacity.

description: NOS3 encodes endothelial nitric oxide synthase (eNOS), which produces NO in vascular endothelium. NO is a potent vasodilator that improves skeletal muscle blood flow during exercise, enhances oxygen delivery, and reduces vascular resistance. The rs2070744 T-786C promoter variant decreases NOS3 transcription, lowering basal and exercise-stimulated NO production.

sources.name: Nakayama M et al. (1999) - T-786C mutation in the 5'-flanking region of the endothelial nitric oxide synthase gene

notes: rs2070744 is often analyzed in haplotype with the Glu298Asp (rs1799983) variant for cardiovascular and athletic phenotypes. NO production is also modulated by dietary nitrate intake. Most studies in cardiovascular or European-ancestry endurance athlete cohorts.

GG.description: Higher baseline aerobic capacity

GA.description: Average aerobic trainability

AA.phenotype: Greater VO2 Max Improvement with Training
AA.description: Greater VO2 max improvement with training

description: PGC-1alpha variant affecting mitochondrial biogenesis and aerobic fitness response.

sources.name: Stefan N et al. (2007) - Genetic variations in PPARD and PPARGC1A determine mitochondrial function and change in aerobic physical fitness and insulin sensitivity during lifestyle intervention

notes: PPARGC1A Gly482Ser variant associated with aerobic fitness response to exercise training. Effect is modest and context-dependent. VO2 max trainability is polygenic — individual genetic variants explain <1% of variation. Training response depends primarily on exercise regimen.

CC.description: Full alpha-actinin-3 expression in fast-twitch muscle fibers, favors power and sprint performance

CT.description: Reduced alpha-actinin-3, balanced power and endurance capacity

TT.description: Complete alpha-actinin-3 deficiency (R577X), muscle fibers shift toward endurance characteristics

description: ACTN3 R577X determines presence of alpha-actinin-3 in fast-twitch muscle fibers. The most replicated sports genetics variant, influencing power vs endurance athletic capacity.
notes: The 577X (T) allele creates a premature stop codon, eliminating alpha-actinin-3. ~18% of humans worldwide are XX (TT). Nearly absent in elite sprinters but enriched in endurance athletes.

CC.description: Homozygous reference (Pro582); standard HIF-1α hydroxylation kinetics with typical oxygen-sensing and aerobic adaptation in response to training.

CT.description: Heterozygous Pro582/Ser582; partial reduction in HIF-1α degradation rate, moderately enhanced hypoxic gene induction and aerobic training adaptation.

TT.description: Homozygous alternate (Ser582); Ser582 HIF-1α resists hydroxylation-mediated degradation, augmenting hypoxic gene responses and potentially amplifying VO2max gains from endurance training.

description: The rs11549465 Pro582Ser missense variant in HIF1A reduces the efficiency of prolyl hydroxylase-mediated HIF-1α degradation under normoxic conditions. This leads to modestly elevated baseline HIF-1α activity, augmenting expression of erythropoietin, VEGF, and glycolytic enzymes. The result is an enhanced angiogenic and erythropoietic response to endurance training that can amplify VO2max improvements.

sources.name: Prabhakar NR et al. (2012) - Hypoxia-inducible factors and athletic performance

notes: The T (Ser582) allele is rare (~3-5% in European populations), limiting statistical power in most studies. VO2max is strongly determined by training history, age, and other genetic factors. This variant should be interpreted in the context of a full aerobic genetics panel.

CC.description: Homozygous reference; standard DAT1 expression supports typical dopamine reuptake kinetics and prefrontal cortex function.

CT.description: Heterozygous; intermediate DAT1 expression with modestly altered dopamine clearance in striatum and prefrontal regions.

TT.description: Homozygous alternate; altered DAT1 function affects dopamine reuptake efficiency in striatal and prefrontal circuits.

description: SLC6A3 encodes the dopamine transporter (DAT1), responsible for clearing dopamine from the synapse in striatum and prefrontal cortex. Variants affecting DAT1 expression alter dopaminergic tone in circuits governing attention and impulse control. rs27072 is a 3'UTR variant influencing DAT1 mRNA stability and has been associated with ADHD susceptibility.

sources.name: Yang B et al. (2007) - Novel variant of the dopamine transporter gene associated with ADHD

notes: ADHD is a heterogeneous neurodevelopmental condition with strong genetic architecture across many loci. This variant represents one modest contributor. ADHD diagnosis requires clinical evaluation; genetic risk scores have limited individual predictive value. This is a risk modifier, not a diagnosis. Consult a healthcare provider for clinical concerns.

CC.description: Homozygous reference; higher 5-HT1A autoreceptor expression increases serotonergic negative feedback, dampening anxiety-circuit firing.

CG.description: Heterozygous; intermediate 5-HT1A expression with variable serotonergic autoinhibition and moderate trait anxiety.

GG.description: Homozygous alternate; reduced 5-HT1A promoter activity lowers autoreceptor density, impairing serotonergic feedback and increasing anxiety-related neural excitability.

description: HTR1A encodes the 5-HT1A serotonin receptor, which functions as both a presynaptic autoreceptor in raphe nuclei and a postsynaptic receptor in limbic regions. The rs6295 C(-1019)G variant disrupts a transcription repressor binding site, reducing 5-HT1A autoreceptor expression and impairing serotonergic feedback inhibition, a mechanism implicated in anxiety and depression.

sources.name: Lesch KP et al. (2003) - Anxiety-related aggression due to epistasis between the 5-HT1A gene and catechol O-methyltransferase

notes: Trait anxiety is a dimensional personality trait; the G allele is associated with reduced 5-HT1A autoreceptor binding in PET studies. Anxiety disorders involve many additional genetic and environmental factors. This is a risk modifier, not a diagnosis.

AA.description: Altered CACNA1C calcium channel expression, associated with increased calcium channel signaling variance and cross-disorder psychiatric susceptibility

AG.description: Moderate CACNA1C effect, slightly elevated calcium channel activity variance

GG.description: Standard CACNA1C expression, population-average calcium channel signaling

description: CACNA1C encodes the alpha-1C subunit of L-type voltage-gated calcium channels. One of the most replicated psychiatric GWAS hits, associated with bipolar disorder and schizophrenia.
notes: CACNA1C is a cross-disorder psychiatric risk gene. The risk allele has been linked to enhanced amygdala activation and increased prefrontal cortex activity during emotional processing. Bipolar disorder is a complex condition influenced by hundreds of genetic variants plus environmental factors. This single variant confers a very small increase in statistical risk. This is a risk modifier, not a diagnosis. Consult a healthcare provider for mental health concerns.

GG.description: Strong morning preference (early bird)

GA.description: Intermediate chronotype

AA.description: Strong evening preference (night owl)

description: Circadian rhythm variation determining natural preference for morning or evening activity.

sources.name: Jones SE et al. (2019) - Genome-wide association analyses of chronotype in 697,828 individuals provides insights into circadian rhythms

notes: Associated with chronotype (morningness/eveningness) in UK Biobank GWAS. Intergenic locus; nearest genes are not definitively established as causal. Effect is modest — circadian preference is influenced by age, light exposure, work schedules, and many other genetic variants. Part of a highly polygenic trait.

TT.description: Homozygous reference; associated with optimal prefrontal dopamine levels supporting cognitive set-shifting and flexible executive function.

TC.description: Heterozygous; intermediate COMT-influenced dopamine catabolism with moderate cognitive flexibility performance.

CC.description: Homozygous alternate; elevated COMT activity increases prefrontal dopamine catabolism, potentially reducing cognitive flexibility in challenging tasks.

description: COMT (catechol-O-methyltransferase) degrades catecholamines including dopamine in the prefrontal cortex, a critical region for executive function and cognitive flexibility. Variants influencing COMT activity alter the dopamine concentration available to prefrontal D1 receptors. Optimal dopamine levels support cognitive flexibility via an inverted-U relationship.

sources.name: Reuter M et al. (2006) - An fMRI study on dopamine and cognitive flexibility

notes: Cognitive flexibility is a multidimensional construct influenced by many genes, education, and practice. COMT variants interact with stress and working memory load (warrior/worrier model). Effect sizes in healthy populations are typically small to moderate. This is a risk modifier, not a diagnosis. Consult a healthcare provider for clinical concerns.

CC.description: Standard stress cortisol response

CG.description: Slightly enhanced cortisol sensitivity

GG.description: Enhanced cortisol sensitivity - stronger stress response

description: Glucocorticoid receptor variant affecting hypothalamic-pituitary-adrenal axis sensitivity.

sources.name: DeRijk RH et al. (2006) - A common polymorphism in the mineralocorticoid receptor modulates stress responsiveness

notes: Moderate evidence for NR3C1 variant's role in cortisol stress response. This is one of multiple contributing genetic factors. Environmental and lifestyle factors also play important roles.

GG.description: Normal deep sleep duration

GA.description: Enhanced deep sleep quality

AA.description: Significantly more deep sleep

description: Adenosine deaminase variant affecting deep sleep duration and sleep quality.

sources.name: Retey JV et al. (2005) - A functional genetic variation of adenosine deaminase affects the duration and intensity of deep sleep in humans

notes: ADA variant (Asp8Asn) associated with increased slow-wave (deep) sleep. One of the few replicated single-gene effects on sleep architecture. Carriers of the Asn allele show ~30 minutes more deep sleep. Rare variant (~5-8% in Europeans).

CC.description: Homozygous reference; lower HTR2A promoter activity associated with reduced serotonin 2A receptor density in prefrontal cortex and limbic regions.

CT.description: Heterozygous; intermediate HTR2A expression with moderate serotonergic tone in cortical regions.

TT.description: Homozygous alternate; higher HTR2A promoter activity elevates 5-HT2A receptor levels in prefrontal cortex, associated with increased serotonergic receptor density.

description: HTR2A encodes the 5-HT2A serotonin receptor, a key mediator of cortical serotonin signaling and a target of antidepressant drugs. The rs6311 promoter variant alters HTR2A transcription, influencing receptor density in the prefrontal cortex and limbic system. Higher expression has been linked to increased vulnerability to depression and altered antidepressant response.

sources.name: Spurlock G et al. (1998) - A family based association study of T102C polymorphism in 5HT2A and psychiatric disorder

notes: Depression is a complex disorder influenced by dozens of genes plus strong environmental factors (stress, trauma, social support). This is a risk modifier, not a diagnosis. T allele frequency is approximately 40-50% in European populations. Consult a healthcare provider for clinical concerns.

CC.description: Homozygous reference; lower DAT1 expression slows synaptic dopamine reuptake, prolonging dopaminergic signal duration in striatum.

CG.description: Heterozygous; intermediate DAT1 expression with moderate dopamine clearance kinetics.

GG.description: Homozygous alternate; higher DAT1 expression accelerates synaptic dopamine reuptake, shortening reward signal duration.

description: SLC6A3 encodes the dopamine transporter (DAT1), the primary mechanism for terminating dopaminergic neurotransmission in striatal and prefrontal synapses. This intronic variant influences DAT1 expression levels, directly setting the kinetics of dopamine clearance. Faster clearance shortens reward signal duration while slower clearance extends it.

sources.name: Heinz A et al. (2000) - Relationship between dopamine transporter availability and DAT1 genotype

notes: Dopamine clearance rate interacts with COMT genotype and environmental stimulation to determine net prefrontal dopamine tone. The SLC6A3 10-repeat VNTR is the most studied DAT1 variant; rs3756577 is in partial LD with it. Not predictive of specific outcomes.

GG.description: Homozygous reference; higher TPH2 promoter activity supports greater serotonin synthesis in the raphe nuclei, associated with lower amygdala reactivity to emotional stimuli.

GA.description: Heterozygous; intermediate serotonin synthesis capacity, with variable emotional reactivity across situations.

AA.description: Homozygous alternate; reduced TPH2 promoter activity lowers brain serotonin production, associated with heightened amygdala responses and greater emotional sensitivity.

description: TPH2 encodes tryptophan hydroxylase 2, the rate-limiting enzyme for serotonin biosynthesis in the brain. The rs4570625 variant lies in the promoter and modulates TPH2 transcription, directly influencing synaptic serotonin availability. Lower serotonin tone is associated with heightened limbic reactivity and emotional sensitivity in neuroimaging studies.

sources.name: Canli T et al. (2005) - A functional polymorphism in the serotonin biosynthetic enzyme TPH2 that is associated with limbic emotional reactivity

notes: Emotional reactivity is shaped by many genetic and environmental factors; this variant contributes a modest portion of variance. Results have been replicated in fMRI studies but effect sizes are small. Not predictive of psychiatric diagnoses.

AA.description: Reduced oxytocin sensitivity, less empathetic behavior, more stress reactivity

AG.description: Intermediate oxytocin signaling and social behavior

GG.description: Enhanced oxytocin sensitivity, more empathetic, better social skills, lower stress

description: Oxytocin receptor variant associated with empathy, social behavior, and stress resilience. GG genotype linked to more prosocial behavior.

sources.name: Rodrigues SM et al. (2009) - Oxytocin receptor genetic variation relates to empathy and stress reactivity in humans

notes: OXTR variant widely studied for associations with empathy, social behavior, and stress reactivity. Effect sizes are small and findings have been inconsistent in replication attempts. Social behavior is overwhelmingly shaped by environment; this SNP has minimal predictive value for any individual.

GG.description: Typical fear extinction

GA.description: Slightly slower fear extinction

AA.description: Stronger fear conditioning and slower extinction

description: Cannabinoid receptor variant affecting amygdala-based fear learning and anxiety.

sources.name: Chakrabarti B et al. (2006) - Variation in the human cannabinoid receptor CNR1 gene modulates gaze duration for happy faces

notes: CNR1 cannabinoid receptor variant studied for associations with anxiety and fear response. Evidence is preliminary with small effect sizes. Fear response is a complex neurobiological phenotype with major environmental and experiential determinants.

CC.description: Strong right-hand preference

CT.description: Mixed or ambidextrous tendency

TT.description: Left-hand preference more likely

description: Proprotein convertase variant influencing left-right body asymmetry and handedness.

sources.name: Brandler WM et al. (2013) - Common variants in left/right asymmetry genes and pathways are associated with relative hand skill

notes: PCSK6 variant associated with handedness in individuals with dyslexia. Effect on handedness in the general population is very small. Handedness is ~25% heritable with the rest due to prenatal environment and chance. No single gene determines handedness.

GG.description: Homozygous reference; higher TPH1 activity supports adequate peripheral serotonin production and typical serotonergic tone.

GA.description: Heterozygous; intermediate TPH1-driven serotonin synthesis with typical variation in serotonergic modulation.

AA.description: Homozygous alternate; reduced TPH1 activity lowers peripheral serotonin synthesis, affecting serotonergic modulation of limbic circuits.

description: TPH1 encodes tryptophan hydroxylase 1, the rate-limiting enzyme for serotonin synthesis in peripheral tissues and pineal gland. The rs1800532 variant in the TPH1 intron 7 region affects mRNA expression and has been associated with impulsivity and aggression through reduced serotonergic modulation of limbic circuits. Central serotonin deficiency is a well-established correlate of impulsive aggression.

sources.name: Manuck SB et al. (2005) - Aggression and anger-related traits associated with a polymorphism of the tryptophan hydroxylase gene

notes: Impulsivity and aggression are strongly shaped by upbringing, social context, and co-occurring conditions. The association with rs1800532 has been replicated but with modest effect sizes. TPH2 (brain-specific) is more directly relevant to central serotonin. This is a risk modifier, not a diagnosis.

CC.description: Tendency toward extraversion

CT.description: Balanced introversion/extraversion

TT.description: Tendency toward introversion

description: Gene variant associated with personality trait dimensions in large GWAS studies.

sources.name: de Moor MHM et al. (2012) - Meta-analysis of genome-wide association studies for personality

notes: Associated with personality traits in a large GWAS meta-analysis but with very small effect size. Personality is overwhelmingly shaped by environment and experience. Individual genetic variants contribute negligibly to personality prediction. Included for research interest only.

CC.description: Lower bruxism tendency

CT.description: Moderate clenching risk

TT.description: Higher jaw clenching/teeth grinding tendency

description: Serotonin receptor variant associated with sleep bruxism and jaw clenching.

sources.name: Rintakoski K et al. (2012) - Bruxism is associated with nicotine dependence: a nationwide Finnish twin cohort study

notes: HTR2A serotonin receptor variant studied in context of bruxism and various psychiatric conditions. Evidence for bruxism association is preliminary. Serotonin receptor genetics influence many behaviors — single-SNP prediction is unreliable.

CC.description: Normal BDNF secretion, efficient hippocampal plasticity, better episodic memory

CT.description: Reduced activity-dependent BDNF secretion, slightly lower hippocampal efficiency

TT.description: Impaired activity-dependent BDNF secretion, reduced hippocampal-dependent memory

description: BDNF Val66Met (rs6265) affects activity-dependent secretion of brain-derived neurotrophic factor, the most important neurotrophin for hippocampal learning and memory.
notes: Val66Met impairs intracellular BDNF trafficking and activity-dependent secretion, but not constitutive secretion. Met carriers show reduced hippocampal volume, impaired episodic memory, and increased anxiety-related behavior.

GG.description: Standard pitch perception

GA.description: Enhanced pitch discrimination

AA.description: Superior pitch perception ability

description: Vasopressin receptor variant associated with musical aptitude and pitch recognition.

sources.name: Ukkola LT et al. (2009) - Musical aptitude is associated with AVPR1A-haplotypes

notes: AVPR1A variant studied for associations with musical aptitude and social cognition. Evidence is preliminary with small sample sizes. Musical ability is overwhelmingly shaped by training and environment — genetic contributions are minimal and polygenic.

GG.description: Lower nicotine dependence risk

GA.description: Moderate risk

AA.description: Higher risk of nicotine dependence

description: Nicotinic receptor variant affecting susceptibility to tobacco addiction.

sources.name: Saccone SF et al. (2007) - Cholinergic nicotinic receptor genes implicated in a nicotine dependence association study targeting 348 candidate genes with 3713 SNPs

notes: CHRNA5 variant strongly associated with nicotine dependence and smoking heaviness (~1 extra cigarette/day per risk allele). Also linked to lung cancer risk through increased smoking. One of the most robust addiction genetics findings. Risk allele frequency ~35% in Europeans.

TT.description: Homozygous reference; standard DRD4 upstream regulation associated with moderate dopaminergic tone and typical novelty-seeking behavior.

TC.description: Heterozygous; intermediate DRD4 expression modulation, associated with moderately elevated openness to new experiences.

CC.description: Homozygous alternate; altered DRD4 transcriptional regulation linked to higher dopamine-driven reward-seeking and exploratory behavior.

description: DRD4 encodes the dopamine D4 receptor, which modulates prefrontal cortex dopamine signaling involved in reward, motivation, and exploratory behavior. The rs1800955 promoter variant influences DRD4 transcription, affecting receptor density and dopaminergic reward sensitivity. Higher DRD4 activity has been associated with novelty and sensation seeking in personality studies.

sources.name: Benjamin J et al. (1996) - Population and familial association between the D4 dopamine receptor gene and measures of Novelty Seeking

notes: Novelty seeking is highly polygenic and shaped by environment, culture, and life experience. This is a single-locus contribution to a complex trait with large non-genetic variance. Effect sizes in published studies are typically small (r ~0.1). Not a predictor of specific behaviors.

CC.description: Normal D2 receptor density, less impulsive, lower addiction risk

CT.description: Intermediate dopamine signaling and behavioral traits

TT.description: Reduced D2 receptor density (Taq1A), more novelty seeking, impulsivity, addiction susceptibility

description: ANKK1 variant (formerly called DRD2 Taq1A) affecting dopamine D2 receptor expression. T allele associated with ~30% reduction in receptor density.

sources.name: Noble EP et al. (1998) - D2 dopamine receptor polymorphism and brain regional glucose metabolism

notes: TaqIA polymorphism near DRD2, associated with dopamine receptor density. Originally linked to novelty seeking and addiction risk, but meta-analyses show small and inconsistent effects. Behavioral genetics effects are highly polygenic — single-SNP predictions are unreliable.

CC.description: Homozygous reference; associated with higher striatal DRD2 expression, supporting stronger dopaminergic reward signaling and motivation.

CT.description: Heterozygous; moderate DRD2 expression with typical variation in reward sensitivity and motivational drive.

TT.description: Homozygous alternate; reduced DRD2 availability in striatum associated with blunted reward signals and higher risk for anhedonia.

description: DRD2 encodes the dopamine D2 receptor, the primary mediator of dopaminergic reward signaling in the striatum. The rs2283265 intronic variant affects DRD2 splicing and receptor isoform ratios, altering striatal D2 receptor density and influencing reward prediction, reinforcement learning, and motivational salience.

sources.name: Zhang Y et al. (2007) - Functional SNPs in the 5-flanking region of the DRD2 gene

notes: DRD2 variants have been widely studied in addiction, schizophrenia, and obesity research. This variant's effect on general reward processing is modest and highly context-dependent. Not predictive of any specific disorder.

GG.description: Lower snoring tendency

TT.description: Higher tendency for snoring

description: Klotho protein variant associated with respiratory muscle tone during sleep.

sources.name: Campos AI et al. (2020) - Genome-wide association study of snoring identifies 42 loci and shared genetic architecture with distinct sleep and respiratory traits

notes: KLOTHO variant (KL-VS) primarily studied for aging and cognitive function. Association with snoring is indirect and based on limited evidence. Snoring is overwhelmingly influenced by BMI, sleeping position, nasal anatomy, and alcohol use.

GG.description: Homozygous reference; associated with higher OXTR expression and greater responsiveness to oxytocin, supporting prosocial behavior and stronger social bond formation.

GA.description: Heterozygous; intermediate oxytocin receptor function with typical variation in social responsiveness.

AA.description: Homozygous alternate; reduced OXTR responsiveness associated with lower empathy scores and reduced preference for social interaction in some studies.

description: OXTR encodes the oxytocin receptor, mediating oxytocin's role in social cognition, bonding, trust, and empathy. The rs2254298 intronic variant is in linkage disequilibrium with functional promoter regions affecting receptor expression. The G allele has been associated with higher prosocial behavior and parent-infant bonding quality.

sources.name: Chen FS et al. (2011) - Common oxytocin receptor gene (OXTR) polymorphism and social support interact to reduce incidence of depression

notes: Social bonding is shaped by developmental history, attachment style, and culture in addition to genetics. OXTR variants have been studied in autism spectrum disorder research but effect sizes for individual variants are small. Not predictive of any clinical condition.

GG.description: LA/LA genotype, higher serotonin transporter expression, potentially greater stress resilience

GT.description: Mixed alleles, intermediate serotonin transporter expression and stress response

TT.description: LG/LG genotype, lower serotonin transporter expression, similar to short allele carriers in stress sensitivity

description: SLC6A4 rs25531 subdivides the long (L) allele of the serotonin transporter promoter into LA (high expression) and LG (low expression, functionally similar to the short allele).
notes: This SNP refines the classic 5-HTTLPR serotonin transporter polymorphism. The LG allele has expression similar to the S allele. Gene-environment interaction with stressful life events is debated. Note: The 5-HTTLPR gene-environment interaction failed to replicate in Border et al. 2019 meta-analysis (N=443,264). Individual SNP effects on stress resilience are modest. This is a risk modifier, not a diagnosis. Consult a healthcare provider for clinical concerns.

AC.description: CHEK2 1100delC carrier, ~2-3x increased breast cancer risk, elevated colon and prostate cancer risk

AA.description: Homozygous CHEK2 1100delC, substantially increased risk of multiple cancer types

CC.description: Wild-type CHEK2, population-average cancer susceptibility

description: CHEK2 1100delC is a frameshift mutation that truncates checkpoint kinase 2, a critical DNA damage response protein. Moderate-penetrance multi-cancer susceptibility allele.
notes: CHEK2 1100delC is a moderate-penetrance cancer allele particularly prevalent in Northern/Eastern Europeans (~1-2%). Confers ~2-3x breast cancer risk. Also increases prostate, colorectal, and thyroid cancer risk. Genetic counseling is recommended for carriers.

CC.description: Full OGG1 glycosylase activity, efficient removal of 8-oxoguanine lesions

CG.description: Intermediate OGG1 activity, slightly reduced 8-oxoguanine repair

GG.description: Lower OGG1 efficiency, impaired 8-oxoguanine removal, modestly increased cancer susceptibility

description: OGG1 Ser326Cys variant affects 8-oxoguanine DNA glycosylase, the primary enzyme for repairing oxidative DNA damage. Impaired repair increases mutagenesis and cancer risk.
notes: 8-oxoguanine is the most common oxidative DNA lesion (~1000/cell/day). The Cys326 variant has reduced repair capacity. Particularly relevant for lung cancer risk in smokers. Genetic counseling is recommended for carriers.

CC.description: Homozygous reference; normal ATM kinase catalytic function supports full activation of the DNA damage response cascade upon double-strand break detection.

CT.description: Heterozygous; one variant allele may moderately reduce ATM kinase efficiency, with partial attenuation of downstream DDR signaling.

TT.description: Homozygous alternate; variant associated with lower ATM kinase activity, potentially reducing the fidelity of the DNA damage checkpoint response.

description: ATM kinase is activated by DNA double-strand breaks and orchestrates the entire DNA damage response through phosphorylation of hundreds of substrates. The rs1800056 variant in the ATM coding sequence has been studied as part of the ATM variant landscape associated with cancer predisposition, distinct from the rs1800057 (Pro1054Arg) locus. Altered ATM kinase activity may impair apoptotic signaling in damaged cells.

sources.name: Bernstein et al. (2006) - ATM sequence variants and cancer risk

notes: Multiple ATM variants contribute cumulatively to DSB repair capacity. This is a risk factor, not a diagnosis. Consult a healthcare provider. Genetic counseling is recommended for interpretation.

GG.description: Homozygous reference; Asp1853 ATM isoform with standard kinase activation kinetics, autophosphorylation, and downstream substrate engagement upon DSB detection.

GA.description: Heterozygous (Asp/Asn1853); one variant allele may moderately alter ATM kinase activation dynamics or interaction with MRN complex components.

AA.description: Homozygous alternate (Asn1853); Asp1853Asn substitution within the ATM HEAT domain may affect kinase activation by the MRN-RPA signaling complex.

description: ATM (Ataxia Telangiectasia Mutated) encodes a PIKK family serine/threonine kinase that is the master regulator of the DNA double-strand break response. The rs1801516 variant encodes an Asp1853Asn substitution in the ATM HEAT repeat domain, which is important for protein-protein interactions within the DSB signaling complex. This variant has been studied as a moderate-penetrance cancer susceptibility allele, particularly for breast cancer.

sources.name: Bernstein et al. (2006) - ATM sequence variants and cancer risk

notes: ATM rs1801516 (Asp1853Asn) is a moderate-penetrance variant studied in BRCA-negative breast cancer families. This is a risk factor, not a diagnosis. Consult a healthcare provider. Genetic counseling is recommended for interpretation.

CC.description: Homozygous reference; standard BCL2 anti-apoptotic activity balances cell survival and mitochondrial pathway apoptosis under normal physiological conditions.

CA.description: Heterozygous; one variant allele may moderately alter BCL2 expression, affecting the balance between pro- and anti-apoptotic BCL2 family members.

AA.description: Homozygous alternate; variant associated with altered BCL2 expression levels, potentially affecting mitochondrial membrane permeabilization threshold and cell survival.

description: BCL2 encodes the founding member of the BCL-2 family of apoptosis regulators, functioning as an anti-apoptotic guardian of mitochondrial outer membrane integrity. BCL-2 sequesters pro-apoptotic BAX and BAK proteins to prevent cytochrome c release and caspase activation. The rs2279115 variant in the BCL2 promoter/regulatory region influences expression levels. Elevated BCL2 is a hallmark of follicular lymphoma (t(14;18) translocation) and contributes to chemotherapy resistance.

sources.name: Nuckel H et al. (2007) - BCL2 gene polymorphism and outcome in chronic lymphocytic leukemia

notes: BCL2 overexpression is a cancer hallmark; this germline variant has modest population-level effect vs. somatic alterations. This is a risk factor, not a diagnosis. Consult a healthcare provider. Genetic counseling is recommended for interpretation.

AA.description: Homozygous ATM variant, significantly impaired DNA damage response and elevated multi-cancer risk

AT.description: ATM carrier, mildly increased breast and other cancer risk (~2x)

TT.description: Wild-type ATM, normal DNA damage checkpoint signaling

description: ATM (Ataxia Telangiectasia Mutated) kinase is the master regulator of the DNA double-strand break response. Heterozygous carriers have moderately increased cancer risk.
notes: ATM is a serine/threonine kinase activated by DNA double-strand breaks. Homozygous loss causes ataxia-telangiectasia; heterozygous carriers (~1% of population) have ~2x breast cancer risk. Genetic counseling is recommended for carriers.

CC.description: Homozygous reference; standard MUTYH function removes adenine mispaired with 8-oxoguanine lesions, preventing G:C to T:A transversions.

CA.description: Heterozygous; one Gln338His allele may partially reduce MUTYH substrate recognition or catalysis, with moderately elevated oxidative mutation burden.

AA.description: Homozygous alternate (Gln338His); variant associated with reduced MUTYH adenine glycosylase activity, increasing risk of accumulating 8-oxoguanine-induced mutations.

description: MUTYH encodes MutY homolog, a DNA glycosylase in the base excision repair pathway that removes adenine misincorporated opposite 8-oxoguanine (a major oxidative DNA lesion). Defective MUTYH activity allows G:C to T:A transversion mutations to accumulate, particularly in colorectal tissue. Biallelic pathogenic MUTYH mutations cause MUTYH-associated polyposis (MAP); common variants like rs3219489 have more modest effects.

sources.name: Kuno T et al. (2012) - Alterations of the base excision repair gene MUTYH in sporadic colorectal cancer

notes: MUTYH biallelic pathogenic mutations cause MAP syndrome; rs3219489 is a common coding variant with modest effect. This is a risk factor, not a diagnosis. Consult a healthcare provider. Genetic counseling is recommended for interpretation.

AA.description: Impaired XRCC1 function, reduced base excision repair, modestly increased cancer susceptibility

AG.description: Heterozygous Arg399Gln, slightly reduced repair capacity

GG.description: Full XRCC1 Arg399 function, efficient base excision repair

description: XRCC1 Arg399Gln affects the BRCT1 domain critical for protein-protein interactions in base excision repair. The most studied XRCC1 polymorphism for cancer susceptibility.
notes: Arg399Gln is in the BRCT1 domain that interacts with PARP1 and poly(ADP-ribose). The Gln allele reduces repair efficiency after oxidative damage. Gln allele frequency ~35% in Europeans, ~25% in East Asians. Genetic counseling is recommended for carriers.

AA.description: Homozygous 2q35 risk variant, modestly increased breast cancer susceptibility

AG.description: Heterozygous, slightly increased breast cancer risk

GG.description: Non-risk genotype, population-average breast cancer susceptibility

description: 2q35 variant in an intergenic region associated with breast cancer susceptibility. One of multiple common low-penetrance breast cancer risk loci identified by GWAS.
notes: 2q35 is one of the first GWAS-identified breast cancer susceptibility loci. Effect size is modest (OR ~1.1-1.2 per allele) but highly replicated. Risk allele frequency ~50% in Europeans. Genetic counseling is recommended for carriers.

AG.description: Heterozygous 8q24 risk variant, slightly increased breast cancer susceptibility

AA.description: Non-risk genotype at 8q24 locus, population-average breast cancer risk

GG.description: Homozygous 8q24 risk variant, modestly increased breast cancer susceptibility

description: 8q24 gene desert variant near MYC oncogene associated with breast cancer susceptibility through long-range enhancer regulation of MYC expression.
notes: 8q24 harbors multiple independent cancer risk loci affecting different cancer types. This variant specifically associates with breast cancer. The region contains enhancer elements that loop to contact the MYC promoter. Genetic counseling is recommended for carriers.

CC.description: Wild-type BRCA2 at this position, population-average cancer risk

CT.description: BRCA2 pathogenic variant carrier, significantly increased breast, ovarian, and other cancer risks

TT.description: Homozygous BRCA2 pathogenic variant, very high cancer susceptibility

description: BRCA2 pathogenic variant disrupting DNA double-strand break repair via homologous recombination. Carriers have substantially elevated lifetime breast, ovarian, prostate, and pancreatic cancer risk.
notes: BRCA2 pathogenic variants confer ~45-55% lifetime breast cancer risk and ~15-25% ovarian cancer risk. Also increases male breast cancer, prostate, and pancreatic cancer risk. PARP inhibitors are effective targeted therapy. Genetic counseling is recommended for carriers.

AA.description: Homozygous reference at the FGFR2 intron 2 locus. No elevated breast cancer risk from this variant; baseline risk applies.

AG.description: Heterozygous carrier of the G risk allele within FGFR2. Associated with a moderately increased risk for estrogen-receptor-positive breast cancer in GWAS discovery cohorts.

GG.description: Homozygous G allele; both copies carry the risk-associated FGFR2 intronic variant, conferring the highest per-locus relative risk in this region in population studies.

description: rs1219648 is an intronic variant within FGFR2 (fibroblast growth factor receptor 2) on chromosome 10q26. FGFR2 is a receptor tyrosine kinase that mediates fibroblast growth factor signaling critical for mammary gland development and is overexpressed in a subset of breast cancers. The G allele at rs1219648 disrupts binding sites for transcription factors including OCT-1/RUNX2, increasing FGFR2 expression in breast tissue and driving proliferation in response to estrogen-related signaling pathways.

sources.name: Easton DF et al. (2007) - Genome-wide association study identifies novel breast cancer susceptibility loci

notes: Genetic counseling is recommended for interpretation. This represents a statistical risk factor identified through population studies. This is not a diagnosis. rs1219648 in FGFR2 is among the most replicated common breast cancer susceptibility loci. Effect is predominantly on estrogen-receptor-positive (ER+) breast cancer. This variant alone does not replace clinical risk assessment tools (e.g., Tyrer-Cuzick model) or genetic testing for BRCA1/BRCA2.

CC.description: Homozygous reference at the MAP3K1 locus. No elevated breast cancer risk conferred by this variant; baseline lifetime risk applies.

CA.description: Heterozygous carrier of the A risk allele. Carries a moderately increased statistical risk for estrogen-receptor-positive breast cancer, consistent with per-allele odds ratios reported in GWAS studies.

AA.description: Homozygous A allele; both copies carry the risk-associated variant in the MAP3K1 region, associated with the highest relative risk at this locus in population-based studies.

description: rs889312 is an intronic variant near MAP3K1 (mitogen-activated protein kinase kinase kinase 1) on chromosome 5q11. MAP3K1 encodes a serine/threonine kinase that acts as a central node in the MAPK/ERK and JNK signaling cascades regulating cell proliferation and survival. GWAS analyses identified the A allele as associated with increased susceptibility to breast cancer, particularly estrogen-receptor-positive subtypes, likely through altered regulatory elements influencing MAP3K1 expression in mammary tissue.

sources.name: Easton DF et al. (2007) - Genome-wide association study identifies novel breast cancer susceptibility loci

notes: Genetic counseling is recommended for interpretation. This represents a statistical risk factor identified through population studies. This is not a diagnosis. The A allele at rs889312 confers a modest per-allele odds ratio (~1.1); effect size is small compared to BRCA1/BRCA2 pathogenic variants. Polygenic risk scores combining multiple loci provide better risk stratification than any single common variant.

CT.description: Heterozygous TOX3 risk variant, moderately increased breast cancer susceptibility

CC.description: Non-risk genotype, population-average breast cancer risk

TT.description: Homozygous TOX3 risk variant, ~1.4x increased breast cancer risk

description: TOX3/TNRC9 16q12 variant is one of the strongest common breast cancer susceptibility loci. TOX3 is a chromatin remodeling factor affecting estrogen-responsive gene regulation.
notes: TOX3 variant has stronger effect in ER-positive breast cancer. Risk allele frequency ~25% in Europeans. TOX3 regulates chromatin structure at estrogen-responsive enhancers. Genetic counseling is recommended for carriers.

CC.description: Wild-type BRCA1 at this position, population-average cancer risk

CT.description: BRCA1 variant carrier, significantly increased lifetime breast and ovarian cancer risk

TT.description: Homozygous BRCA1 variant, very high cancer susceptibility

description: BRCA1 missense variant associated with increased susceptibility to hereditary breast and ovarian cancer syndrome. BRCA1 is a critical tumor suppressor involved in DNA double-strand break repair.
notes: BRCA1 pathogenic variants confer 60-80% lifetime breast cancer risk and 20-40% ovarian cancer risk. Genetic counseling and enhanced screening recommended for carriers.

AA.description: No K3326X variant, population-average BRCA2-related cancer risk

AT.description: Carrier of BRCA2 K3326X truncation, modestly increased cancer susceptibility

TT.description: Homozygous BRCA2 K3326X, increased susceptibility to breast and other cancers

description: BRCA2 K3326X is a truncating variant that removes the last 93 amino acids. Associated with moderately increased breast, ovarian, and lung cancer risk.
notes: K3326X is a common BRCA2 truncation (1-2% carrier frequency) with moderate cancer risk. Unlike classic BRCA2 pathogenic variants, the risk increase is more modest (~2-fold for breast cancer). Genetic counseling is recommended for carriers.

CC.description: Homozygous reference; standard CASP8 expression supports efficient extrinsic apoptotic pathway activation in response to death receptor signaling.

CT.description: Heterozygous; one variant allele may moderately reduce CASP8 expression, with intermediate apoptotic pathway activation upon death receptor stimulation.

TT.description: Homozygous alternate; variant associated with lower CASP8 expression, potentially reducing extrinsic apoptosis efficiency and tumor immune surveillance.

description: CASP8 encodes caspase-8, the initiator caspase of the extrinsic (death receptor) apoptotic pathway, activated by TRAIL, FasL, and TNF signaling. Caspase-8 processes downstream effector caspases-3 and -7 to execute apoptosis. Reduced caspase-8 expression may impair immune-mediated tumor cell killing. Variants in the CASP8 promoter region have been associated with altered cancer susceptibility in population studies.

sources.name: Sun T et al. (2007) - A six-nucleotide insertion-deletion polymorphism in the CASP8 promoter is associated with susceptibility to multiple cancers

notes: Reduced caspase-8 impairs immune-mediated tumor killing; effect varies by cancer type and exposure. This is a risk factor, not a diagnosis. Consult a healthcare provider. Genetic counseling is recommended for interpretation.

AA.description: Homozygous CHEK2 I157T, substantially increased breast, colon, and prostate cancer risk

AG.description: CHEK2 I157T carrier, ~1.5x increased breast cancer risk, modest increase in other cancers

GG.description: Wild-type CHEK2, population-average cancer susceptibility

description: CHEK2 I157T missense variant impairs checkpoint kinase 2 function in DNA damage response. Moderate-penetrance breast cancer susceptibility allele also linked to colorectal and prostate cancer.
notes: CHEK2 I157T is a moderate-penetrance cancer allele. More common in Northern Europeans (~5%). Risk is lower than BRCA1/2 but higher than common GWAS hits. Also increases colorectal and prostate cancer risk. Genetic counseling is recommended for carriers.

GG.description: Homozygous 8q24 risk variant, ~1.5x increased colorectal and prostate cancer risk

GT.description: Heterozygous, ~1.2x increased colorectal and prostate cancer risk

TT.description: Non-risk genotype, population-average colorectal and prostate cancer risk

description: 8q24 variant near the MYC oncogene enhances a long-range enhancer element. The most consistently replicated common genetic risk factor for both colorectal and prostate cancer.
notes: 8q24 is a gene desert, but this variant sits in an enhancer element that physically contacts the MYC promoter 335kb away via chromatin looping. Risk allele frequency ~50%. TCF4 binding site affected by the variant. Genetic counseling is recommended for carriers.

CC.description: Homozygous reference at the 20p12 locus. No elevated colorectal cancer risk conferred by this variant; population-average risk applies.

CA.description: Heterozygous carrier of the A risk allele at 20p12. Associated with a moderate increase in colorectal cancer susceptibility in GWAS discovery and replication cohorts.

AA.description: Homozygous A allele; both copies carry the risk-associated 20p12 variant, associated with the highest per-locus relative risk for colorectal cancer at this region.

description: rs961253 is an intergenic variant at chromosome 20p12 identified through GWAS as a colorectal cancer susceptibility locus. The region lacks an immediately obvious candidate gene, but chromatin interaction data suggests the variant may regulate BMP2 (bone morphogenetic protein 2) expression, a key inhibitor of colonic epithelial proliferation in the BMP signaling pathway. Dysregulation of BMP2 has been documented in colorectal adenomas and carcinomas, providing a plausible biological mechanism for the observed association.

sources.name: Broderick P et al. (2007) - A genome-wide association study shows that common alleles of SMAD7 influence colorectal cancer risk

notes: Genetic counseling is recommended for interpretation. This represents a statistical risk factor identified through population studies. This is not a diagnosis. rs961253 was part of a multi-locus GWAS discovery at SMAD7/BMP pathway regions. Individual variant effect size is modest; actionable clinical decisions should be based on aggregate polygenic risk combined with family history and lifestyle factors.

TT.description: Homozygous reference; no elevated risk from this GREM1 upstream variant. Gremlin-1 expression at baseline levels suppresses BMP signaling normally.

TC.description: Heterozygous carrier of the C risk allele. Associated with moderately increased colorectal cancer susceptibility, consistent with additive upregulation of GREM1 expression and BMP pathway suppression.

CC.description: Homozygous C allele; both copies carry the risk variant associated with the highest relative colorectal cancer risk at this locus. GREM1 over-expression results in sustained BMP pathway suppression in colonic epithelium.

description: rs4779584 lies upstream of GREM1 (gremlin 1), a BMP (bone morphogenetic protein) antagonist on chromosome 15q13. Gremlin-1 binds and inhibits BMP2, BMP4, and BMP7, which normally suppress colonic crypt cell proliferation and promote differentiation. The C risk allele at rs4779584 is associated with elevated GREM1 expression, leading to chronic suppression of BMP signaling in the colorectal epithelium, promoting adenoma formation and progression to carcinoma. Rare high-penetrance duplications of GREM1 cause hereditary mixed polyposis syndrome, placing this gene squarely in colorectal cancer biology.

sources.name: Broderick P et al. (2007) - A genome-wide association study shows that common alleles of SMAD7 influence colorectal cancer risk

notes: Genetic counseling is recommended for interpretation. This represents a statistical risk factor identified through population studies. This is not a diagnosis. Allele frequency varies markedly across populations: the T (reference) allele reaches ~80% in East Asians versus ~17% in some European cohorts. Population context is critical for interpreting this variant's contribution to an individual's colorectal cancer risk.

GG.description: Homozygous reference at the 10p14 locus. No elevated colorectal cancer risk conferred by this variant; baseline population risk applies.

GA.description: Heterozygous carrier of the A risk allele at 10p14. Associated with a moderate increase in colorectal cancer susceptibility consistent with the per-allele effect observed in GWAS discovery and replication cohorts.

AA.description: Homozygous A allele; both copies carry the risk-associated variant at 10p14, associated with the highest relative colorectal cancer risk at this locus in population studies.

description: rs10795668 is located in the 10p14 chromosomal region, identified through genome-wide association studies of colorectal cancer as an independent susceptibility locus. The variant lies in an intergenic region and is thought to act through long-range regulatory mechanisms, potentially influencing the expression of neighboring genes involved in Wnt pathway signaling or epithelial cell homeostasis in colonic tissue. The A allele was associated with increased colorectal adenoma and cancer risk in European-ancestry discovery cohorts.

sources.name: Broderick P et al. (2007) - A genome-wide association study shows that common alleles of SMAD7 influence colorectal cancer risk

notes: Genetic counseling is recommended for interpretation. This represents a statistical risk factor identified through population studies. This is not a diagnosis. The per-allele odds ratio for rs10795668 is modest (~1.12); clinical utility is greatest when incorporated into polygenic risk scores combining multiple colorectal cancer susceptibility loci. Standard colonoscopy screening is recommended per national guidelines regardless of genotype.

AA.description: Homozygous reference at the 8q24 locus. No elevated risk conferred by this variant; baseline colorectal cancer risk applies.

AG.description: Heterozygous carrier of the G risk allele at 8q24. Carries a moderately increased statistical risk for colorectal cancer, consistent with the additive effect observed in GWAS populations.

GG.description: Homozygous G allele at 8q24; both copies carry the risk-associated variant. Population studies show this genotype is associated with the highest relative risk at this locus among the three genotype classes.

description: rs10505477 is located in the 8q24 chromosomal region — a gene-poor 'gene desert' that nonetheless harbors multiple independent colorectal cancer risk loci. The G allele was identified in genome-wide association studies as conferring increased susceptibility to colorectal cancer through long-range regulatory interactions with oncogenes such as MYC. This variant is one of several independent signals within 8q24 that collectively contribute to inherited colorectal cancer susceptibility across diverse populations.

sources.name: Tomlinson I et al. (2007) - A genome-wide association scan of tag SNPs identifies a susceptibility variant for colorectal cancer at 8q24.21

notes: Genetic counseling is recommended for interpretation. This represents a statistical risk factor identified through population studies. This is not a diagnosis. The G allele frequency varies across ancestries (approximately 39–49% globally). Multiple independent signals exist at 8q24; cumulative polygenic risk models are more informative than any single variant. Colonoscopy screening guidelines should be followed independent of genotype.

CC.description: Homozygous reference; standard ATM kinase activity enables efficient phosphorylation of H2AX, BRCA1, and CHK2 in response to double-strand breaks.

CG.description: Heterozygous; one Pro1054Arg allele may partially reduce ATM kinase signaling efficiency, with moderately attenuated DNA damage response.

GG.description: Homozygous alternate; Pro1054Arg substitution may impair ATM kinase activation or substrate recognition, reducing DSB signaling fidelity.

description: ATM (Ataxia Telangiectasia Mutated) encodes a serine/threonine kinase that is the master sensor and signal transducer of DNA double-strand breaks. Upon DSB detection, ATM autophosphorylates and phosphorylates hundreds of substrates including H2AX, BRCA1, CHK2, and p53 to coordinate DNA repair, cell cycle arrest, and apoptosis. The rs1800057 (Pro1054Arg) variant has been studied in relation to cancer predisposition.

sources.name: Bernstein et al. (2006) - ATM sequence variants and cancer risk

notes: ATM missense variants confer moderate cancer risk; distinguished from rare ATM loss-of-function mutations. This is a risk factor, not a diagnosis. Consult a healthcare provider. Genetic counseling is recommended for interpretation.

GG.description: Lower ERCC2 helicase activity, impaired nucleotide excision repair, increased cancer susceptibility

GT.description: Moderate ERCC2 function, slightly reduced DNA repair efficiency

TT.description: Full ERCC2/XPD helicase activity, efficient nucleotide excision repair

description: ERCC2/XPD Lys751Gln variant affects the DNA helicase component of nucleotide excision repair (NER). NER removes bulky DNA adducts from UV damage and chemical carcinogens.
notes: ERCC2/XPD is part of the TFIIH transcription/repair complex. The Gln751 variant reduces repair of UV and chemical DNA damage. Particularly relevant for lung cancer risk in smokers. Genetic counseling is recommended for carriers.

CC.description: Full XRCC1 R194W activity, efficient base excision repair of DNA damage

CT.description: Intermediate XRCC1 activity, slightly reduced base excision repair efficiency

TT.description: Lower XRCC1 efficiency, impaired base excision repair, modestly increased cancer susceptibility

description: XRCC1 R194W variant affects base excision repair (BER) pathway efficiency. XRCC1 scaffolds multiple BER enzymes and its variants modify susceptibility to DNA damage-induced cancers.
notes: XRCC1 R194W (Arg194Trp) is more common in East Asian populations (20-30%) than Europeans (5-10%). The variant allele may actually be protective in some contexts, with study results being mixed. Genetic counseling is recommended for carriers.

AA.description: Homozygous reference; standard XRCC2 RAD51-paralogue activity supports efficient homologous recombination of double-strand DNA breaks.

AG.description: Heterozygous; partial alteration in XRCC2 expression or function may moderately reduce HR repair efficiency at double-strand break sites.

GG.description: Homozygous alternate; variant associated with reduced XRCC2 functional contribution to RAD51-mediated strand invasion during homologous recombination.

description: XRCC2 functions as a RAD51 paralogue within the BCDX2 complex to mediate homologous recombination repair of DNA double-strand breaks. This rs3218550 variant is distinct from the coding rs3218536 (Arg188His) variant and is located in a regulatory region that may affect XRCC2 expression levels. Both XRCC2 variants have been studied in breast and ovarian cancer risk contexts.

sources.name: Webb et al. (2005) - XRCC2 polymorphisms and breast cancer risk

notes: rs3218550 is a separate XRCC2 locus from rs3218536; both affect HR repair capacity. This is a risk factor, not a diagnosis. Consult a healthcare provider. Genetic counseling is recommended for interpretation.

AA.description: Homozygous reference; lower EGF promoter activity results in reduced circulating EGF levels and baseline EGFR pathway stimulation.

AG.description: Heterozygous; intermediate EGF production with moderate EGFR activation in target tissues including hepatocytes and epithelial cells.

GG.description: Homozygous alternate (61G/G); higher EGF secretion and greater EGFR pathway stimulation may promote cell proliferation in susceptible tissues.

description: EGF encodes epidermal growth factor, a potent mitogenic ligand for the EGFR (ErbB1) receptor tyrosine kinase that promotes cell proliferation, survival, and migration. Elevated EGF levels chronically stimulate the EGFR-RAS-ERK and PI3K-AKT proliferative signaling cascades. The rs4444903 (A61G) variant in the EGF 5' UTR influences EGF mRNA translation efficiency and circulating protein levels.

sources.name: Bhatt et al. (2004) - EGF promoter polymorphism and hepatocellular carcinoma risk

notes: Higher EGF levels most studied in hepatocellular and glioblastoma contexts; interacts with chronic liver disease. This is a risk factor, not a diagnosis. Consult a healthcare provider. Genetic counseling is recommended for interpretation.

CC.description: Standard CYP1B1 activity, normal estrogen metabolism

CG.description: Moderately increased CYP1B1 activity, slightly elevated genotoxic estrogen metabolites

GG.description: Increased CYP1B1 catalytic activity, elevated genotoxic estrogen metabolites, higher hormone-related cancer risk

description: CYP1B1 Leu432Val variant increases enzyme activity for converting estradiol to 4-hydroxyestradiol, a genotoxic catechol estrogen implicated in breast and endometrial cancer.
notes: CYP1B1 catalyzes estrogen 4-hydroxylation, producing catechol estrogens that form DNA adducts. The Val432 variant has ~3x higher catalytic efficiency for estradiol oxidation. Genetic counseling is recommended for carriers.

CC.phenotype: No FAP Risk from This Variant
CC.description: Wild-type APC gene at this position, no increased polyposis risk

CT.description: Heterozygous APC mutation carrier, high risk of colorectal adenomatous polyps and cancer

TT.description: Homozygous APC mutation, very high risk of early-onset colorectal cancer

description: APC tumor suppressor gene mutation causing familial adenomatous polyposis (FAP), characterized by hundreds to thousands of colorectal adenomas with near-certain cancer progression if untreated.
notes: APC mutations are responsible for FAP, an autosomal dominant condition with near 100% penetrance for colorectal cancer by age 40-50 without intervention. Prophylactic colectomy is standard of care. Genetic counseling is recommended for carriers.

CC.description: Normal FGFR2 regulation, population-average breast cancer risk

CT.description: Intermediate FGFR2 expression, ~1.2x increased breast cancer risk

TT.description: Higher FGFR2 expression, ~1.4x increased ER-positive breast cancer risk

description: FGFR2 intron 2 variant increases fibroblast growth factor receptor 2 expression, the strongest common breast cancer susceptibility locus identified by GWAS.
notes: FGFR2 is the strongest common breast cancer risk locus (OR ~1.26 per allele). Risk allele frequency ~38%. Effect is stronger for ER-positive breast cancer. FGFR2 amplification also drives some cancers. Genetic counseling is recommended for carriers.

AG.description: Heterozygous ERBB2 coding variant, moderately increased breast cancer susceptibility

AA.description: Wild-type ERBB2 at this position, population-average cancer risk

GG.description: Homozygous ERBB2 variant, increased HER2 signaling and breast cancer susceptibility

description: ERBB2 (HER2) Ile655Val coding variant affects receptor tyrosine kinase activity. HER2 overexpression drives ~20% of breast cancers and is a major therapeutic target.
notes: ERBB2 I655V in the transmembrane domain affects receptor dimerization. The Val allele may enhance HER2 signaling. HER2-targeted therapies (trastuzumab, pertuzumab) have transformed HER2+ breast cancer treatment. Genetic counseling is recommended for carriers.

GG.description: Homozygous reference; standard XRCC2 function supports efficient repair of DNA double-strand breaks via homologous recombination, maintaining genomic stability.

GA.description: Heterozygous; one copy of the Arg188His variant moderately reduces XRCC2 RAD51 paralogue function, with intermediate DSB repair capacity.

AA.description: Homozygous alternate; the Arg188His substitution impairs XRCC2 interaction with RAD51, reducing homologous recombination repair fidelity.

description: XRCC2 encodes a RAD51 paralogue essential for homologous recombination (HR)-mediated repair of DNA double-strand breaks. It forms a complex with XRCC3 and other RAD51 paralogues to facilitate strand invasion and repair template annealing. The rs3218536 A allele produces an Arg188His substitution that has been associated with modestly elevated cancer risk in several studies.

sources.name: Webb et al. (2005) - XRCC2 Arg188His polymorphism and risk of breast cancer

notes: XRCC2 variants affect DNA repair capacity; associated with elevated cancer risk in some studies. This is a risk factor, not a diagnosis. Consult a healthcare provider. Genetic counseling is recommended for interpretation.

GG.description: Homozygous reference at the TERT-CLPTM1L locus. No elevated lung cancer risk from this variant; population-average risk applies.

GA.description: Heterozygous carrier of the A risk allele in the TERT-CLPTM1L region. Associated with a moderate increase in lung cancer susceptibility independent of smoking history in discovery cohorts.

AA.description: Homozygous A allele; both copies carry the risk-associated variant at 5p15.33, associated with the highest relative lung cancer risk at this locus across multiple GWAS replication studies.

description: rs4975616 maps to chromosome 5p15.33, a locus containing two key cancer-susceptibility genes: TERT (telomerase reverse transcriptase) and CLPTM1L (cleft lip and palate transmembrane protein 1-like). TERT maintains telomere length enabling cellular immortality — a hallmark of cancer — while CLPTM1L promotes survival signaling in pre-malignant cells. GWAS studies identified variants at this locus as associated with multiple cancer types including lung, bladder, and others, with the A allele linked to altered TERT expression or CLPTM1L activity that reduces apoptotic surveillance.

sources.name: Hung RJ et al. (2008) - A susceptibility locus for lung cancer maps to nicotinic acetylcholine receptor subunit genes on 15q25

notes: Genetic counseling is recommended for interpretation. This represents a statistical risk factor identified through population studies. This is not a diagnosis. Smoking remains the dominant lung cancer risk factor; genetic susceptibility at TERT-CLPTM1L modifies but does not replace the overwhelming contribution of tobacco exposure. The A allele frequency is approximately 52–58% globally. Non-smokers with elevated polygenic risk should discuss low-dose CT screening eligibility with their healthcare provider.

AA.description: Reduced MLH1 promoter activity, increased risk of colorectal, endometrial, and other Lynch syndrome cancers

AG.description: Moderately reduced MLH1 expression, somewhat elevated mismatch repair-related cancer risk

GG.description: Full MLH1 promoter activity, normal DNA mismatch repair capacity

description: MLH1 promoter variant affects expression of a key DNA mismatch repair gene. Reduced MLH1 expression increases microsatellite instability and Lynch syndrome-associated cancer risk.

sources.name: Raptis S et al. (2007) - MLH1 -93G>A promoter polymorphism and microsatellite-unstable colorectal cancer risk

notes: MLH1 -93G>A promoter variant reduces transcription factor binding. The A allele is associated with MLH1 promoter methylation and microsatellite instability in tumors. Frequency ~25% in Europeans. Genetic counseling is recommended for carriers. This is a risk factor identified in research, not a clinical diagnosis. Consult a healthcare provider.

AG.description: MLH1 pathogenic variant carrier, high lifetime risk of colorectal, endometrial, and other cancers

AA.description: Wild-type MLH1, normal mismatch repair function

GG.description: Homozygous MLH1 pathogenic variant, very high multi-cancer susceptibility. This genotype is extremely rare; confirm with clinical-grade genetic testing.

description: MLH1 pathogenic variant causing Lynch syndrome (hereditary nonpolyposis colorectal cancer). MLH1 is a key DNA mismatch repair protein; deficiency causes microsatellite instability.

sources.name: Li S et al. (2017) - Pooling-analysis on hMLH1 polymorphisms and cancer risk

notes: Lynch syndrome carriers have ~80% lifetime CRC risk, ~60% endometrial cancer risk, and elevated ovarian, gastric, and urinary tract cancer risk. MSI-high tumors respond to anti-PD-1 immunotherapy. Genetic counseling is recommended for carriers. This is a risk factor identified in research, not a clinical diagnosis. Consult a healthcare provider. Penetrance estimates are primarily derived from European-ancestry cohorts and may differ in other populations.

AG.description: Heterozygous MLH1 coding variant, potential impairment of DNA mismatch repair

AA.description: Wild-type MLH1, normal DNA mismatch repair function

GG.description: Homozygous MLH1 variant, reduced mismatch repair capacity, increased hereditary cancer risk

description: MLH1 coding variant affecting DNA mismatch repair. MLH1 deficiency causes microsatellite instability and Lynch syndrome with increased colorectal, endometrial, and other cancer risks.

sources.name: Chen H et al. (2015) - Association between MutL homolog 1 polymorphisms and colorectal cancer risk: a meta-analysis

notes: MLH1 I219V is a common coding variant. Lynch syndrome carriers have ~80% lifetime colorectal cancer risk. MMR-deficient tumors respond well to immune checkpoint inhibitors (anti-PD-1). Genetic counseling is recommended for carriers. This is a risk factor identified in research, not a clinical diagnosis. Consult a healthcare provider. Penetrance estimates are primarily derived from European-ancestry cohorts and may differ in other populations.

AG.description: BRCA1 missense variant carrier, potential modifier of breast/ovarian cancer risk

AA.description: Wild-type BRCA1 at this position, standard cancer susceptibility

GG.description: Homozygous BRCA1 variant, possible effect on DNA repair efficiency

description: BRCA1 missense variant that may modify DNA repair capacity. BRCA1 is critical for homologous recombination repair of DNA double-strand breaks.
