GG.description: Homozygous reference; standard CTLA4 co-inhibitory signaling that downregulates T-cell activation. Associated with baseline autoimmune thyroid disease risk (~58% G globally).

GA.description: Heterozygous; intermediate CTLA4 3'UTR regulatory context. One A allele is associated with moderately reduced CTLA4 expression and intermediate susceptibility to Graves' disease and Hashimoto's thyroiditis.

AA.description: Homozygous alternate; the A allele (~42% globally, ~45% European, ~60% South Asian) is associated with reduced CTLA4 mRNA levels via altered 3'UTR regulation, permitting greater T-cell autoreactivity against thyroid antigens.

description: CTLA4 (cytotoxic T-lymphocyte antigen 4, CD152) encodes a co-inhibitory receptor on T cells that competes with CD28 for B7 ligand binding, downregulating T-cell activation and preventing autoimmunity. rs3087243 is a 3'UTR variant that reduces CTLA4 mRNA stability and expression. The A allele has been consistently associated with autoimmune thyroid diseases (Graves' disease and Hashimoto's thyroiditis) as well as type 1 diabetes, celiac disease, and rheumatoid arthritis across multiple populations. CTLA4-Ig (abatacept) is an approved immunotherapy that exploits this pathway.

sources.name: Ueda H et al. (2003) - Association of the T-cell regulatory gene CTLA4 with susceptibility to autoimmune disease

notes: This is a risk factor, not a diagnosis. The A allele is common (~42% globally) with notable enrichment in South Asians (~60%) and lower frequency in Africans (~23%). The association with Graves' disease and Hashimoto's thyroiditis is one of the most replicated in autoimmune genetics (OR ~1.3-1.5 per allele). Individuals with AA genotype and family history of thyroid disease, type 1 diabetes, or other autoimmune conditions should discuss thyroid function monitoring (TSH, anti-TPO, anti-TG) with their healthcare provider. CTLA4 variations affect multiple autoimmune conditions, not solely thyroid disease.

CC.description: Standard catalase expression, normal hydrogen peroxide detoxification

CT.description: Mildly reduced catalase promoter activity, slightly lower antioxidant defense

TT.description: Lower catalase expression, reduced hydrogen peroxide clearance capacity

description: CAT -262C>T promoter variant affects catalase expression, the primary enzyme for converting hydrogen peroxide to water and oxygen in cellular antioxidant defense.
notes: Catalase is a key antioxidant enzyme. The T allele reduces promoter activity. Combined with SOD2 and GPX1 variants, contributes to overall oxidative stress defense capacity.

CC.description: Baseline FOXO3 activity, population-average aging trajectory

CT.description: Intermediate FOXO3 expression, slightly enhanced stress resistance and longevity potential

TT.description: Increased FOXO3 activity, enhanced cellular stress resistance and associated with exceptional longevity

description: FOXO3 variant in the insulin/IGF-1 signaling pathway. FOXO3 transcription factor regulates stress resistance, apoptosis, and autophagy. One of the most replicated human longevity genes.
notes: FOXO3 is the only gene besides APOE consistently replicated for human longevity across multiple populations. It mediates the longevity effects of caloric restriction and insulin signaling.

CC.description: Homozygous reference allele associated with baseline FOXO3 transcriptional activity and typical cellular resistance to oxidative and proteotoxic stress.

CT.description: Heterozygous carriers show intermediate FOXO3 pathway activation; partial enrichment in long-lived cohorts.

TT.description: Homozygous alternate allele linked to greater FOXO3 nuclear localization under stress, stronger activation of autophagy and antioxidant gene programs, and enrichment in centenarians.

description: FOXO3 encodes a forkhead box transcription factor that activates genes controlling oxidative stress resistance, apoptosis, DNA repair, and autophagy. Multiple intronic FOXO3 variants including rs2153960 have been independently replicated across multiple populations as the strongest known longevity-associated genetic locus.

sources.name: Willcox et al. (2008) - FOXO3 and human longevity

notes: FOXO3 is the most replicated longevity locus across diverse populations. Fasting, exercise, and caloric restriction activate FOXO3 independently of genotype. This is a risk factor, not a diagnosis.

GG.description: Homozygous reference allele associated with typical FOXO3 haplotype and standard cellular aging trajectory in population studies.

GA.description: Heterozygous carriers show partial enrichment in long-lived cohorts; FOXO3-mediated stress protection is moderately enhanced.

AA.description: Homozygous alternate allele is part of the FOXO3 haplotype block associated with centenarian status and healthy aging in Japanese and European populations.

description: The rs1935949 variant lies within the FOXO3 intronic region and is part of the haplotype block most strongly associated with exceptional longevity. This haplotype is thought to influence FOXO3 regulatory element function, enhancing stress-responsive transcription of autophagy, antioxidant defense, and cell cycle arrest genes.

sources.name: Willcox et al. (2008) - FOXO3 haplotype and longevity

notes: Longevity genetic associations are probabilistic across populations; individual outcomes depend heavily on lifestyle and environment. This is a risk factor, not a diagnosis.

CC.description: Homozygous reference allele associated with standard FOXO3 regulatory element function and typical transcriptional response to stress and aging stimuli.

CT.description: Heterozygous carriers show partial enrichment in healthy aging cohorts; intermediate FOXO3 pathway activity under cellular stress conditions.

TT.description: Homozygous alternate allele in the FOXO3 longevity haplotype block; associated with enrichment in nonagenarians and centenarians in multiple cohorts.

description: The rs2764264 variant is part of the FOXO3 longevity-associated haplotype block and influences the transcriptional regulation of FOXO3 target genes involved in cellular homeostasis, proteostasis, and stress resistance. This locus has been replicated in multiple independent aging cohorts worldwide.

sources.name: Willcox et al. (2008) - FOXO3 regulatory variants and aging

notes: FOXO3 variants are among the most robustly replicated human longevity loci. Lifestyle interventions that activate FOXO3 include fasting, exercise, and antioxidant-rich diet. This is a risk factor, not a diagnosis.

GG.description: Increased FOXO3 activity, better cellular stress resistance, enriched in centenarians

GT.description: Intermediate FOXO3 expression, moderate stress resistance benefit

TT.description: Baseline FOXO3 activity, population-average aging and stress response

description: FOXO3 longevity-associated variant affecting the forkhead box O3 transcription factor. Independently replicated across Japanese, German, Italian, Chinese, and American centenarian cohorts.
notes: FOXO3 variants regulate autophagy, oxidative stress resistance, and apoptosis. The longevity allele may increase FOXO3 nuclear localization and transcriptional activity. Effect size ~1.5x for reaching 100 years.

CC.description: Homozygous reference; strongly associated with spontaneous clearance of hepatitis C virus infection (~60-80% clearance in CC individuals). The C allele prevents IFNL4 (IFN-lambda-4) protein production, paradoxically improving IFN signaling response. C allele ~72% in Europeans, ~40% in Africans.

CT.description: Heterozygous; intermediate probability of spontaneous HCV clearance. One T allele allows partial IFNL4 expression, partially dampening IFN-alpha/beta signaling efficacy. Also shows intermediate response to peginterferon/ribavirin treatment.

TT.phenotype: Lower HCV Clearance — Chronic Infection Risk
TT.description: Homozygous alternate; strongly associated with failure to clear HCV spontaneously and chronification. The T allele induces IFNL4 protein which paradoxically impairs downstream IFN-alpha/beta signaling. T allele ~60% in Africans, ~28% in Europeans, ~5% in East Asians.

description: IFNL4 encodes interferon lambda-4, a type III interferon discovered in 2013. Paradoxically, the T allele at rs12979860 creates a functional IFNL4 protein that impairs — rather than enhances — the hepatic antiviral response by inducing ISG (interferon-stimulated gene) expression that desensitizes cells to subsequent interferon-alpha/beta signaling. The C allele disrupts IFNL4 open reading frame, preventing this protein and allowing more effective IFN signaling. This explains the long-observed association between this locus and both spontaneous HCV clearance and treatment response to interferon-based therapies.

sources.name: Thomas DL et al. (2009) - Genetic variation in IL28B and spontaneous clearance of hepatitis C virus

notes: This is one of the most clinically validated pharmacogenomic loci in hepatology. The counterintuitive biology (a gene that makes an interferon but hurts interferon responses) makes this a fascinating teaching example in genetics. Strong population stratification: C allele ~72% European, ~40% African, ~95% East Asian — reflecting different evolutionary HCV exposure histories. In the era of direct-acting antivirals (DAAs), this variant no longer affects treatment selection for HCV, but it remains clinically relevant for understanding spontaneous clearance probability and for contexts where interferon therapy is still used. Discuss HCV testing and treatment options with an infectious disease or hepatology specialist.

TT.description: Homozygous reference (T/T) at the IL28B locus. Strongly associated with spontaneous HCV clearance and favorable response to pegylated interferon-alpha plus ribavirin therapy; approximately 2–3 times higher sustained virologic response (SVR) rates compared to G carriers.

TG.description: Heterozygous T/G genotype; one favorable T allele and one unfavorable G allele. Intermediate probability of sustained virologic response to interferon-based HCV therapy; response rates fall between TT and GG genotype groups.

GG.description: Homozygous G allele; both copies carry the unfavorable genotype associated with reduced interferon-stimulated gene induction and lower probability of SVR to pegylated interferon-based regimens for HCV genotype 1.

description: rs8099917 is located near IFNL3 (interferon lambda 3, also known as IL-28B) on chromosome 19q13, a gene encoding type III interferon that initiates antiviral innate immune responses through the JAK-STAT pathway. The T allele at rs8099917 is in strong linkage disequilibrium with a 4-nucleotide insertion/deletion upstream of IFNL4 that affects IFNL3 and IFNL4 expression levels. Higher hepatic IFNL3 expression in T allele carriers drives stronger interferon-stimulated gene induction, improving the innate immune response to HCV infection and enhancing the pharmacodynamic effect of exogenous pegylated interferon therapy.

sources.name: Ge D et al. (2009) - Genetic variation in IL28B predicts hepatitis C treatment-induced viral clearance

notes: This is a risk factor, not a diagnosis. Consult a healthcare provider. rs8099917 genotype was clinically validated as a pre-treatment predictor of sustained virologic response (SVR) to pegylated interferon + ribavirin for HCV genotype 1. However, with the advent of direct-acting antivirals (DAAs) achieving >95% SVR irrespective of IL28B genotype, clinical utility of this test has decreased substantially for DAA-eligible patients. The G allele is more common in individuals of African ancestry, contributing to historically lower interferon treatment response rates in that population.

TT.description: Homozygous reference allele associated with lower basal IL-1 beta transcription and reduced NLRP3 inflammasome-driven chronic inflammation.

TC.description: Heterozygous carriers show intermediate IL-1B promoter activity; moderately elevated inflammasome-driven aging risk.

CC.description: Homozygous alternate allele linked to higher IL-1 beta promoter activity, driving greater inflammasome-mediated chronic low-grade inflammation associated with accelerated aging.

description: The rs1143627 variant in the IL1B promoter region alters transcription factor binding and modulates baseline IL-1 beta expression. Elevated IL-1B is a key driver of inflammaging, the chronic low-grade sterile inflammation that accelerates biological aging and increases susceptibility to age-related diseases.

sources.name: Pociot et al. (2003) - IL1B promoter and inflammation

notes: IL-1B-driven inflammaging is modifiable by Mediterranean diet, regular exercise, and metformin in some populations. This is a risk factor, not a diagnosis.

TT.description: Homozygous reference allele associated with greater IL-1 receptor antagonist levels, stronger anti-inflammatory counterbalancing of IL-1 signaling.

TC.description: Heterozygous carriers show intermediate IL-1RA production; partial attenuation of IL-1-mediated inflammatory aging.

CC.description: Homozygous alternate allele linked to lower IL-1RA levels, less effective counterbalancing of IL-1 beta activity, and greater chronic inflammation susceptibility.

description: IL1RN encodes the IL-1 receptor antagonist, which competitively inhibits both IL-1 alpha and IL-1 beta binding to their receptor, acting as a key brake on inflammatory cascades. Variants reducing IL-1RA production impair immunoregulation and have been associated with inflammatory diseases and longevity phenotypes.

sources.name: Pociot et al. (2003) - IL1RN and inflammatory balance

notes: IL-1RA is also a therapeutic protein (anakinra) used in autoinflammatory disease. Genetic variation in IL1RN affects susceptibility to arthritis and infections. This is a risk factor, not a diagnosis.

CC.description: Increased anti-inflammatory IL-10 expression, potentially beneficial for longevity and reduced chronic inflammation

CT.description: Moderate IL-10 levels, balanced inflammatory regulation

TT.description: Reduced anti-inflammatory IL-10, potentially stronger inflammatory responses

description: IL-10 -1082A>G promoter variant affects production of interleukin-10, the most important anti-inflammatory cytokine. High IL-10 is associated with longevity and reduced age-related disease.
notes: IL-10 suppresses inflammatory cytokine production. High IL-10 producers are enriched among centenarians. The anti-inflammatory genotype may protect against atherosclerosis and neurodegeneration.

AA.description: Homozygous reference allele associated with greater IL-10 promoter activity, higher anti-inflammatory cytokine production, and enriched representation in centenarian cohorts.

AG.description: Heterozygous carriers show intermediate IL-10 levels; immune aging trajectory is between AA and GG.

GG.description: Homozygous alternate allele linked to reduced IL-10 transcription, less immunoregulation, and potentially faster inflammaging with age.

description: IL10 encodes interleukin-10, the primary immunoregulatory cytokine that suppresses pro-inflammatory responses. The rs1800871 (-819C/T in older nomenclature) promoter variant is part of the IL-10 haplotype block associated with longevity in Italian and Danish centenarian studies, linking higher IL-10 to reduced inflammaging.

sources.name: Lio et al. (2002) - IL10 and centenarians

notes: IL-10 is part of the immunotype associated with exceptional longevity. Anti-inflammatory diet and moderate exercise support IL-10 production independently. This is a risk factor, not a diagnosis.

TT.description: Homozygous reference allele associated with higher IL-1RA expression in monocytes and macrophages, linked to favorable immunosenescence in longevity studies.

TC.description: Heterozygous carriers show intermediate IL-1RA production; immune aging profile is between the two homozygous states.

CC.description: Homozygous alternate allele linked to lower IL-1RA in aging immune cells, potentially contributing to greater inflammaging and less favorable longevity trajectory.

description: The rs419598 variant in IL1RN influences monocyte IL-1RA production. In centenarian association studies, higher IL-1RA alleles correlate with maintained immune balance in extreme old age by counteracting the pro-inflammatory cytokine environment characteristic of inflammaging.

sources.name: Pociot et al. (2003) - IL1RN and longevity

notes: IL-1RN variants interact with IL-1B variants (Kornman composite genotype) to determine net inflammatory tone. This is a risk factor, not a diagnosis.

GG.description: Homozygous reference; standard IL-10 expression. IL-10 is an anti-inflammatory cytokine; GG genotype is associated with moderate anti-inflammatory capacity and standard inflammatory bowel disease risk (~86% globally).

GA.description: Heterozygous; one A allele is associated with slightly reduced IL-10 anti-inflammatory activity. Intermediate risk for inflammatory conditions including IBD and systemic lupus erythematosus.

AA.description: Homozygous alternate; A allele (~14% globally) associated with lower IL-10 transcription, potentially allowing greater pro-inflammatory responses. Elevated risk for inflammatory bowel disease (ulcerative colitis, Crohn's), and autoimmune conditions in susceptible backgrounds.

description: IL10 encodes interleukin-10, a pleiotropic anti-inflammatory cytokine produced by T regulatory cells, macrophages, and dendritic cells that suppresses pro-inflammatory cytokine production (TNF-alpha, IL-6, IL-12). rs3024505 is located near the IL10 gene 3' end and is strongly associated with inflammatory bowel disease susceptibility in GWAS studies. The A allele is associated with reduced IL-10 expression, which could shift the immune balance toward pro-inflammatory signaling in the gut and systemically.

sources.name: Franke A et al. (2010) - Genome-wide meta-analysis increases to 71 the number of confirmed Crohn's disease susceptibility loci

notes: This is a risk factor, not a diagnosis. The A allele (~14% globally) shows notable population stratification: European ~16%, Asian ~3-12%, African ~5%. The OR for IBD per A allele is modest (~1.2-1.4), indicating this is one of many contributing loci. Inflammatory bowel disease is a complex polygenic condition requiring clinical evaluation for diagnosis. Individuals with A allele carriage and gastrointestinal symptoms or family history of IBD/autoimmune disease should discuss with a gastroenterologist or physician.

AA.description: Homozygous reference encoding aspartic acid (Asp) at IL6R position 358. Wild-type IL-6 receptor with standard membrane shedding rate; normal circulating soluble IL-6R levels and inflammatory signaling capacity.

AC.description: Heterozygous Asp358Ala carrier. One copy of the Ala allele (C) increases ADAM10/ADAM17 cleavage efficiency at the IL-6R ectodomain; intermediate elevation of soluble IL-6R (sIL-6R) with partial trans-signaling effect.

CC.description: Homozygous Ala358 variant; both copies encode the C allele associated with increased IL-6R ectodomain shedding, elevated circulating sIL-6R, and enhanced IL-6 trans-signaling with altered systemic inflammatory tone.

description: rs2228145 is a missense variant in IL6R (interleukin-6 receptor) encoding p.Asp358Ala on chromosome 1q21. The Asp→Ala substitution at position 358, located in the cleavage domain of the IL-6R ectodomain, increases the efficiency of metalloprotease (ADAM10/ADAM17) proteolytic shedding. This generates higher circulating levels of soluble IL-6R (sIL-6R), which can form complexes with IL-6 and activate gp130 signaling on cells that do not express the membrane-bound receptor ('trans-signaling'). The C allele was identified in Mendelian randomization studies as associated with reduced risk for coronary heart disease and rheumatoid arthritis, validating IL-6R as a therapeutic target.

sources.name: Interleukin-6 Receptor Mendelian Randomisation Analysis (IL6R MR) Consortium (2012) - The interleukin-6 receptor as a target for prevention of coronary heart disease

notes: This is a risk factor, not a diagnosis. Consult a healthcare provider. rs2228145 (Asp358Ala) is the functional IL6R variant that genetically mimics tocilizumab (IL-6 receptor antibody) blockade. Mendelian randomization shows the C allele is associated with reduced CRP, fibrinogen, and cardiovascular risk — providing human genetic validation of IL-6 as a cardiovascular drug target. The C allele is common (approximately 30–40% globally). This variant is used in pharmacogenomic research to identify individuals likely to benefit from anti-IL-6 therapy.

AA.description: Homozygous reference; standard TLR4 function with typical lipopolysaccharide (LPS) sensing and downstream NF-kB pro-inflammatory signaling. Most individuals (~94%) carry this genotype.

AG.description: Heterozygous; one G allele (Asp299Gly) reduces TLR4 ectodomain LPS binding affinity. Associated with reduced inflammatory responses to gram-negative bacterial antigens and possible protection from septic shock, but increased susceptibility to gram-negative infections.

GG.description: Homozygous alternate; extremely rare. Both TLR4 receptors carry the Gly299 variant, resulting in substantially impaired LPS sensing. Associated with increased susceptibility to gram-negative bacterial infections. G allele ~6% globally.

description: TLR4 (toll-like receptor 4) is the primary pattern recognition receptor for lipopolysaccharide (LPS) from gram-negative bacteria, forming a co-complex with MD-2 and CD14 to activate NF-kB and IRF3 pro-inflammatory signaling pathways. The rs4986790 variant encodes an Asp299Gly substitution in the TLR4 extracellular domain that reduces LPS binding affinity and downstream signal transduction. This creates a dual phenotype: reduced septic shock risk (lower hyper-inflammatory response) but increased susceptibility to gram-negative infections such as E. coli urinary tract infections and malaria.

sources.name: Arbour NC et al. (2000) - TLR4 mutations are associated with endotoxin hyporesponsiveness in humans

notes: This is a risk factor, not a diagnosis. The G allele (~6% globally) shows strong population stratification: South Asian ~10.5%, African ~7.7%, European ~5.8%, East Asian ~0.08%. The extremely low East Asian frequency suggests this variant arose primarily in non-East-Asian populations. The double-edged nature — protection from septic shock but vulnerability to infection — illustrates the evolutionary trade-off inherent in immune receptor variants. Individuals with the G allele may warrant vigilance regarding gram-negative infections. Discuss with a healthcare provider if frequent urinary or gastrointestinal infections are a concern.

CC.description: Homozygous reference; functional mannose-binding lectin (MBL) with normal oligomerization and complement lectin pathway activation. Standard innate immune defense against pathogens with mannose/N-acetylglucosamine surface patterns. ~93-96% globally.

CT.phenotype: Reduced MBL Levels — Partial MBL Deficiency
CT.description: Heterozygous; one T allele encodes an MBL2 variant with impaired oligomerization. Circulating MBL levels are approximately 50% of normal. Mild increased susceptibility to recurrent infections in infancy and early childhood.

TT.phenotype: Very Low MBL — Functional MBL Deficiency
TT.description: Homozygous alternate; extremely low or absent functional MBL in serum. T allele ~3.5% globally (~22% in Africans, ~0.09% in East Asians). TT genotype is extremely rare globally but found at higher rates in African populations.

description: MBL2 encodes mannose-binding lectin, an oligomeric plasma protein of the collectin family that recognizes carbohydrate patterns on microbial surfaces and activates the lectin complement pathway (via MASP-1/2). The rs1800451 variant (Gly54Asp, exon 1, codon 54) encodes a structural amino acid change that disrupts MBL collagen-like domain oligomerization, preventing formation of high-molecular-weight multimers required for complement activation. Carriers of the T allele have dramatically reduced serum MBL and impaired complement lectin pathway activity, predisposing to recurrent respiratory, gastrointestinal, and fungal infections particularly in young children and immunocompromised individuals.

sources.name: Garred P et al. (1999) - Susceptibility to HIV and progression of AIDS in relation to variant alleles of mannose-binding lectin

notes: This is a risk factor, not a diagnosis. The T allele is extremely rare in East Asians (~0.09%) but common in Africans (~22%), with Europeans intermediate (~1.8%). The high African T allele frequency may reflect selective pressure from endemic infectious diseases where partial MBL deficiency may provide a heterozygous advantage (similar to sickle-cell heterozygosity). MBL deficiency is clinically relevant for immunocompromised individuals, transplant recipients, and infants with recurrent infections — in these contexts, MBL2 genotyping is sometimes performed. Complement pathway tests (CH50, MBL levels) can quantify functional MBL. Discuss with an immunologist if recurrent unexplained infections are a concern.

TT.description: Average longevity tendency

GG.description: Associated with exceptional longevity

description: FOXO3 variant consistently associated with human longevity across multiple populations.

sources.name: Willcox BJ et al. (2008) - FOXO3A genotype is strongly associated with human longevity

notes: FOXO3 variant associated with longevity in multiple populations (European, Japanese, Chinese). G allele carriers show 1.2-1.5x increased odds of reaching 100 years. Mechanism involves improved stress resistance and insulin signaling. Longevity is highly polygenic and environmental.

trait: MHC Region Immune Variant / Heat Shock Protein / HLA Cluster

GG.description: Homozygous reference at the HSPA1A synonymous coding site within the MHC class III region. Standard heat shock protein 70 expression; population-average immune surveillance capacity.

GA.description: Heterozygous carrier. The A allele represents a synonymous change (CAG→CAA, both encoding glutamine) in HSPA1A; functional effect may arise through linkage with flanking HLA haplotypes rather than direct coding impact.

AA.description: Homozygous A allele at the HSPA1A synonymous site. Effect on immune phenotype is primarily through extended MHC haplotype context and co-inherited HLA alleles rather than the synonymous coding change per se.

description: rs1061581 is a synonymous coding variant in HSPA1A (heat shock protein 70 family member 1A, c.1053G>A, p.Gln351Gln) located within the MHC class III region on chromosome 6p21. HSPA1A encodes HSP70-1, a molecular chaperone critical for antigen processing and presentation; HSP70 binds peptides and assists in loading MHC class I molecules, directly supporting CD8+ T cell-mediated immune surveillance. As a synonymous variant, rs1061581's functional effects are likely mediated through mRNA stability, translational efficiency, or strong linkage disequilibrium with functional HLA alleles and other MHC region variants rather than amino acid change.

sources.name: International HIV Controllers Study, Pereyra F et al. (2010) - The major genetic determinants of HIV-1 control affect HLA class I peptide presentation

notes: This is a risk factor, not a diagnosis. Consult a healthcare provider. The A allele is rare globally (approximately 0.7–1.1% in East Asian populations; broader frequency data limited). HSPA1A is embedded in the extended MHC, where high linkage disequilibrium makes it difficult to isolate the functional contribution of this specific synonymous variant from neighboring HLA alleles. Comprehensive HLA typing provides superior resolution for immune-related risk assessment.

AA.description: Homozygous reference allele associated with typical SIRT1 regulatory element function and adequate NAD+-dependent deacetylase expression for metabolic homeostasis.

AC.description: Heterozygous carriers show intermediate SIRT1 regulatory activity; minor metabolic aging differences compared to AA.

CC.description: Homozygous alternate allele linked to altered SIRT1 regulatory function, potentially reducing expression under metabolic stress and aging conditions.

description: The rs7896005 variant lies within a SIRT1 regulatory region influencing enhancer or silencer element activity. As a SIRT1 haplotype tag SNP, it contributes to the composite genetic influence on SIRT1 expression levels and the downstream aging phenotypes mediated by this deacetylase.

sources.name: Guarente et al. (2004) - SIRT1 haplotype and aging

notes: Caloric restriction, resveratrol, and exercise are SIRT1 activators that may compensate for reduced baseline expression. This is a risk factor, not a diagnosis.

CC.description: Homozygous reference allele associated with stronger NAD+-dependent deacetylase activity, enhanced stress response, and favorable longevity-associated metabolic profiles.

CT.description: Heterozygous carriers show intermediate SIRT1 expression; metabolic and aging phenotypes fall between CC and TT individuals.

TT.description: Homozygous alternate allele linked to lower SIRT1 transcription, reduced NAD+ sensing capacity, and potentially less favorable aging trajectory.

description: SIRT1 encodes sirtuin-1, an NAD+-dependent deacetylase that regulates cellular stress responses, DNA repair, mitochondrial biogenesis, and metabolic homeostasis. SIRT1 activity declines with age; variants affecting its expression are associated with healthspan and metabolic aging parameters.

sources.name: Guarente et al. (2004) - SIRT1 and aging

notes: Caloric restriction and NAD+ precursors (NMN, NR) can increase SIRT1 activity regardless of genotype. This is a risk factor, not a diagnosis.

TT.description: Homozygous reference allele associated with higher SIRT1 expression and stronger deacetylase-mediated regulation of aging pathways including p53, FOXO, and PGC-1alpha.

TC.description: Heterozygous carriers show intermediate SIRT1 expression; longevity-associated benefits are partially attenuated.

CC.description: Homozygous alternate allele linked to lower SIRT1 activity, less efficient deacetylation of aging-related substrates, and potentially accelerated metabolic aging.

description: The rs3758391 variant in the SIRT1 promoter region influences transcription factor binding and basal SIRT1 expression levels. SIRT1 deacetylates histones and transcription factors to modulate inflammation, glucose metabolism, and mitochondrial function, all of which contribute to aging trajectories.

sources.name: Guarente et al. (2004) - SIRT1 variants and longevity

notes: SIRT1 rs3758391 is one of several variants in the SIRT1 haplotype block. Effects are additive with lifestyle factors. This is a risk factor, not a diagnosis.

TT.description: Homozygous reference allele associated with more stable COL5A1 mRNA and reduced susceptibility to Achilles tendinopathy and rupture.

TC.description: Heterozygous carriers show intermediate risk for tendon injuries; partial reduction in type V collagen stability.

CC.description: Homozygous alternate allele linked to reduced COL5A1 mRNA stability, weaker tendon matrix, and increased Achilles tendon injury susceptibility.

description: COL5A1 encodes type V collagen, which regulates fibrillogenesis of types I and III collagen in tendons. The rs13946 3'-UTR variant alters mRNA stability, affecting tendon biomechanical properties and injury susceptibility in athletes.

sources.name: September et al. (2009) - COL5A1 and tendon injury

notes: Most studied in endurance athletes. Proper warm-up and eccentric training may reduce injury risk regardless of genotype. This is a risk factor, not a diagnosis.

TT.description: Homozygous reference allele associated with typical tissue-nonspecific alkaline phosphatase activity and normal hydroxyapatite crystal formation in bone.

TC.description: Heterozygous carriers may show subtle changes in ALPL activity, with minor effects on bone matrix calcification efficiency.

CC.description: Homozygous alternate allele linked to lower ALPL enzyme activity, which may impair pyrophosphate metabolism and reduce bone mineral density.

description: ALPL encodes tissue-nonspecific alkaline phosphatase (TNSALP), an enzyme essential for bone and tooth mineralization via pyrophosphate hydrolysis. Coding variants in ALPL cause hypophosphatasia; common variants like rs4236 modulate enzyme activity and are associated with bone mineral density in population studies.

sources.name: Riancho et al. (2013) - ALPL and bone mineral density

notes: Severe ALPL mutations cause hypophosphatasia; this common variant has a much smaller effect. Vitamin D and phosphate status are important environmental modulators. This is a risk factor, not a diagnosis.

GG.description: Homozygous reference at the IL2/IL21 locus. Standard IL-2 and IL-21 cytokine signaling regulation; population-average risk for autoimmune joint inflammatory conditions.

GT.description: Heterozygous carrier of the T protective allele. The T allele at rs6822844 has been associated with reduced risk for type 1 diabetes and rheumatoid arthritis; intermediate effect in heterozygous state.

TT.description: Homozygous T allele; both copies carry the variant associated with the most reduced autoimmune risk at this locus. Associated with lower susceptibility to IL-2/IL-21-driven autoimmune conditions including rheumatoid arthritis.

description: rs6822844 is located in the intergenic region between IL2 (interleukin-2) and IL21 (interleukin-21) on chromosome 4q27, a locus identified in GWAS as associated with multiple autoimmune diseases. IL-2 promotes regulatory T cell (Treg) development and peripheral tolerance, while IL-21 amplifies effector T and B cell responses. Variants at this locus alter the expression balance between these cytokines in immune tissues; the T allele is associated with a protective regulatory effect that reduces pro-inflammatory cytokine output and lowers susceptibility to autoimmune joint inflammation.

sources.name: Smyth DJ et al. (2008) - A genome-wide association study of nonsynonymous SNPs identifies a type 1 diabetes locus in the interferon-induced helicase (IFIH1) region

notes: This is a risk factor, not a diagnosis. Consult a healthcare provider. The T allele at rs6822844 has been associated with reduced risk across multiple autoimmune conditions (type 1 diabetes, rheumatoid arthritis, celiac disease) in European-ancestry GWAS. The T allele frequency is approximately 14% globally. Protection conferred by this variant is partial; other genetic and environmental factors dominate clinical risk for rheumatoid arthritis.

trait: Cartilage Intermediate Layer Protein / Joint Health

TT.description: Homozygous reference allele associated with typical cartilage intermediate layer protein activity and standard intervertebral disc integrity.

TC.description: Heterozygous carriers may show modestly reduced CILP function, with slight susceptibility to cartilage and disc degeneration.

CC.description: Homozygous alternate allele linked to reduced CILP inhibition of TGF-beta signaling and increased intervertebral disc degeneration risk.

description: CILP encodes cartilage intermediate layer protein, which inhibits TGF-beta1 signaling in cartilage and intervertebral discs. Variants disrupting this inhibition lead to abnormal matrix remodeling and are associated with lumbar disc disease and osteoarthritis.

sources.name: Seki et al. (2005) - CILP and disc degeneration

notes: Associated with lumbar disc disease. Postural and ergonomic factors are strong environmental modifiers. This is a risk factor, not a diagnosis.

GG.description: Homozygous reference allele associated with typical COL1A1 transcription, normal collagen I stoichiometry, and standard fracture resistance.

GT.description: Heterozygous carriers may have slightly elevated COL1A1 expression relative to COL1A2, altering collagen fiber mechanics and modestly increasing fracture risk.

TT.description: Homozygous alternate allele linked to overexpression of alpha-1 collagen chains, producing mechanically weaker bone and higher osteoporotic fracture risk.

description: The rs1107946 variant lies in the COL1A1 promoter region and alters Sp1 transcription factor binding, changing the ratio of alpha-1 to alpha-2 collagen chains in bone matrix. This stoichiometric imbalance reduces bone toughness and increases fracture susceptibility.

sources.name: Mann et al. (1997) - COL1A1 Sp1 polymorphism and fracture risk

notes: One of the best-replicated bone density variants. Additive effect with COL1A2 and VDR variants. This is a risk factor, not a diagnosis.

GG.description: Homozygous 1G allele associated with lower MMP1 basal expression and reduced collagen I and III degradation in connective tissues.

GGG.description: Heterozygous carriers show intermediate MMP1 expression; tissue remodeling is moderately elevated compared to 1G homozygotes.

GGGG.description: Homozygous 2G allele linked to higher MMP1 transcription, increased collagen degradation, and greater susceptibility to tendon and ligament injury.

description: MMP1 is a collagenase that cleaves fibrillar collagens I, II, and III. The rs1799750 insertion polymorphism creates an Ets transcription factor binding site in the 2G allele, elevating MMP1 expression and accelerating collagen remodeling in tendons, skin, and joint tissues.

sources.name: Rutter et al. (1998) - MMP1 promoter polymorphism

notes: 2G allele also studied in cardiovascular and cancer contexts. Collagen integrity depends on vitamin C and dietary protein adequacy. This is a risk factor, not a diagnosis. This variant uses simplified nucleotide notation (G/GG) for the -1607 1G/2G insertion polymorphism in the MMP-1 promoter. Note: This insertion/deletion polymorphism may not produce phenotype results from standard DTC genotyping chips, which typically report indels in I/D format rather than nucleotide sequences.

AA.description: ESR1 XbaI XX genotype, associated with slightly higher bone mineral density

AG.description: ESR1 XbaI Xx genotype, intermediate bone mineral density and fracture risk

GG.description: ESR1 XbaI xx genotype, associated with lower bone mineral density and increased fracture risk

description: ESR1 XbaI (IVS1-351A>G) intronic variant modifies estrogen receptor alpha expression. Together with PvuII (rs2234693), forms the classic ESR1 osteoporosis haplotype.
notes: XbaI and PvuII are in LD and form haplotypes. The 'px' haplotype (low-expression) is associated with lower BMD and increased fracture risk. Effect is most pronounced in postmenopausal women.

CC.description: ESR1 PvuII PP genotype, associated with higher bone mineral density in some populations

CT.description: ESR1 PvuII Pp genotype, intermediate bone mineral density effect

TT.description: ESR1 PvuII pp genotype, associated with slightly lower bone mineral density and fracture risk

description: ESR1 PvuII (IVS1-397T>C) intronic variant affects estrogen receptor alpha expression. Estrogen signaling is critical for bone maintenance, and ESR1 variants modify osteoporosis risk.
notes: ESR1 PvuII and XbaI (rs9340799) are classic osteoporosis candidate variants. Meta-analyses show modest but significant effects on BMD. Effect is more pronounced in postmenopausal women not on HRT.

GG.description: Homozygous reference allele associated with stronger estrogen receptor beta signaling and higher bone mineral density in postmenopausal women.

GA.description: Heterozygous carriers show intermediate bone mineral density; modest reduction in estrogenic bone protection compared to GG.

AA.description: Homozygous alternate allele linked to reduced ESR2 activity and greater susceptibility to osteoporosis, particularly after menopause.

description: ESR2 encodes estrogen receptor beta, which modulates osteoblast and osteoclast activity to maintain bone mass. The rs4986938 variant alters receptor expression and has been associated with bone mineral density variation in multiple population studies.

sources.name: Ogawa et al. (2000) - ESR2 and bone mineral density

notes: Risk factor for osteoporosis, especially post-menopause. Effect size is modest and interacts with hormonal status. This is a risk factor, not a diagnosis.

TT.description: Homozygous reference at the TNFRSF11B upstream region. Baseline osteoprotegerin expression and standard bone mineral density trajectory.

TC.description: Heterozygous carrier of the C allele at the TNFRSF11B upstream variant. Associated with moderate alteration in OPG-mediated osteoclast inhibition, potentially influencing bone mineral density and fracture susceptibility.

CC.description: Homozygous C allele; both copies carry the variant associated with altered TNFRSF11B regulatory activity, conferring the most pronounced effect on osteoprotegerin levels and bone remodeling balance at this locus.

description: rs3102735 is a variant approximately 2 kb upstream of TNFRSF11B (tumor necrosis factor receptor superfamily member 11b), which encodes osteoprotegerin (OPG). Osteoprotegerin is a decoy receptor for RANKL (receptor activator of NF-κB ligand) that acts as a critical negative regulator of osteoclastogenesis: by binding RANKL, OPG prevents osteoclast activation and bone resorption. Variants in the TNFRSF11B promoter and 5' regulatory regions influence OPG transcript levels, thereby modulating the RANK-RANKL-OPG axis that governs bone density and fracture risk.

sources.name: Kung AW et al. (2008) - Association of TNFRSF11B gene polymorphisms with bone mineral density in Chinese men

notes: This is a risk factor, not a diagnosis. Consult a healthcare provider. Bone mineral density is a complex polygenic trait; rs3102735 captures only one component of inherited fracture risk. Dietary calcium, vitamin D status, physical activity, and sex hormone levels interact substantially with genetic predisposition. DXA scanning remains the gold standard for clinical fracture risk assessment.

CC.description: Normal GDF5 expression, baseline osteoarthritis risk

CT.description: Reduced GDF5 expression, moderately increased knee and hip osteoarthritis risk

TT.description: Significantly reduced GDF5 expression, higher susceptibility to osteoarthritis

description: GDF5 +104T/C promoter polymorphism reduces expression of growth differentiation factor 5, a key regulator of joint development and cartilage maintenance.
notes: GDF5 is essential for joint and cartilage development. The T allele reduces promoter activity by ~20%. Risk allele is very common (>50% in most populations). Also associated with height variation.

CC.description: Homozygous reference allele associated with stronger GDF5 promoter activity, better cartilage and joint morphogenesis, and lower osteoarthritis risk.

CT.description: Heterozygous carriers show intermediate GDF5 expression and moderately elevated osteoarthritis susceptibility compared to CC individuals.

TT.description: Homozygous alternate allele linked to reduced GDF5 expression, impaired cartilage homeostasis, and significantly elevated knee and hip osteoarthritis risk.

description: GDF5 encodes growth differentiation factor 5, a TGF-beta superfamily member critical for joint formation and cartilage repair. A 5'-UTR promoter variant (rs143384, often tagged by rs926849) reduces GDF5 transcription, impairing chondrocyte differentiation and increasing osteoarthritis susceptibility.

sources.name: Southam et al. (2007) - GDF5 and osteoarthritis

notes: One of the most replicated osteoarthritis susceptibility variants across Asian and European populations. BMI strongly modifies risk. This is a risk factor, not a diagnosis.

AA.description: Impaired LRP5 function, lower bone mineral density and increased fracture risk

AG.description: Mildly impaired Wnt/LRP5 signaling, slightly lower bone mineral density

GG.description: Normal LRP5/Wnt co-receptor function, standard bone mineral density

description: LRP5 variant affecting the Wnt signaling pathway co-receptor. LRP5 is critical for osteoblast differentiation and bone formation, and variants directly influence bone mineral density.
notes: LRP5 mutations span a phenotypic spectrum from high bone mass (gain-of-function) to osteoporosis-pseudoglioma syndrome (loss-of-function). This variant has modest effect on bone density in the general population.

GG.description: Homozygous reference allele associated with baseline MMP3 expression and balanced extracellular matrix degradation in joints.

GA.description: Heterozygous carriers may exhibit modestly increased MMP3 levels, contributing to slightly accelerated cartilage and synovial tissue remodeling.

AA.description: Homozygous alternate allele linked to higher MMP3 expression, increased proteoglycan loss from cartilage, and greater osteoarthritis susceptibility.

description: MMP3 (stromelysin-1) degrades multiple extracellular matrix components including proteoglycans, fibronectin, and collagen. Promoter variants alter MMP3 expression levels, influencing cartilage degradation rates and osteoarthritis progression.

sources.name: Tsezou et al. (1999) - MMP3 and osteoarthritis

notes: MMP3 is also relevant to cardiovascular disease and rheumatoid arthritis. Inflammation management can reduce MMP3 activity. This is a risk factor, not a diagnosis.

GG.description: Normal COL1A1 expression, average bone density and fracture risk

GT.description: Increased COL1A1 transcription, slightly reduced bone mineral density and increased fracture risk

TT.description: Significantly increased COL1A1 expression, lower bone density and higher fracture susceptibility

description: COL1A1 Sp1 binding site polymorphism affects collagen type I alpha-1 chain expression, influencing bone mineral density and osteoporotic fracture risk.
notes: The T allele increases Sp1 transcription factor binding, leading to overproduction of the alpha-1 chain relative to alpha-2, producing weaker bone collagen. Effect is most pronounced in postmenopausal women.

trait: Osteoprotegerin Coding Variant / Fracture Risk (OPG Asn3Lys)

GG.description: Homozygous reference encoding asparagine (Asn) at OPG codon 3. Wild-type osteoprotegerin signal peptide sequence; standard OPG secretion efficiency and RANKL-binding capacity.

GC.description: One copy of the Lys variant (C allele) at OPG codon 3. Heterozygous Asn3Lys change may modestly alter signal peptide processing and OPG secretion efficiency, with intermediate effect on osteoclast inhibition.

CC.description: Homozygous Lys variant; both alleles encode the C-allele lysine substitution at OPG codon 3. Associated with the most pronounced alteration in OPG function and the highest per-locus contribution to altered bone remodeling at this missense site.

description: rs2073618 is a missense variant in TNFRSF11B encoding a p.Asn3Lys substitution in the signal peptide of osteoprotegerin (OPG). The signal peptide directs OPG to the secretory pathway; the lysine (C allele) substitution at position 3 alters the hydrophobic core of the signal sequence, potentially reducing OPG secretion efficiency. Reduced circulating OPG would diminish RANKL neutralization, tilting the RANK-RANKL-OPG balance toward increased osteoclast activity, greater bone resorption, and lower bone mineral density — thereby increasing fracture susceptibility.

sources.name: Kung AW et al. (2008) - Association of TNFRSF11B gene polymorphisms with bone mineral density in Chinese men

notes: This is a risk factor, not a diagnosis. Consult a healthcare provider. rs2073618 is a coding variant with a clear biochemical rationale through OPG signal peptide alteration. However, effect sizes in population studies are modest; comprehensive fracture risk should incorporate additional OPG/RANKL loci, lifestyle factors, and DXA imaging. This variant is distinct from high-penetrance OPG frameshift/truncating mutations associated with juvenile Paget disease.

GG.description: Homozygous reference allele associated with typical RANKL expression and balanced osteoclast-osteoblast activity.

GA.description: Heterozygous carriers may exhibit modestly elevated RANKL signaling, slightly favoring bone resorption over formation.

AA.description: Homozygous alternate allele linked to higher RANKL expression, increased osteoclast activation, and greater bone loss risk.

description: TNFSF11 encodes RANKL (receptor activator of NF-kB ligand), a critical cytokine that promotes osteoclast differentiation and bone resorption. Variants influencing RANKL expression affect the balance of bone remodeling and susceptibility to osteoporosis.

sources.name: Koh et al. (2008) - TNFSF11 and bone mineral density

notes: RANKL is the target of denosumab (osteoporosis drug). Genetic risk interacts with calcium intake and weight-bearing exercise. This is a risk factor, not a diagnosis.

trait: Receptor Activator of NF-kB / Bone Remodeling

CC.description: Homozygous reference allele associated with typical RANK receptor expression and balanced osteoclast activation in response to RANKL.

CT.description: Heterozygous carriers may show modest changes in RANK signaling efficiency, slightly affecting bone resorption rates.

TT.description: Homozygous alternate allele linked to enhanced RANK-RANKL signaling and greater osteoclast activation, increasing bone loss susceptibility.

description: TNFRSF11A encodes RANK, the receptor for RANKL that activates NF-kB signaling in osteoclast precursors. Variants affecting RANK expression or function alter the sensitivity of bone resorption to inflammatory and hormonal cues, influencing osteoporosis risk.

sources.name: Koh et al. (2008) - RANK variants and bone density

notes: RANK pathway is the target of denosumab therapy. Calcium, vitamin D, and exercise counteract excess resorption. This is a risk factor, not a diagnosis.

TT.description: Homozygous reference at the STAT4 intronic locus. Standard JAK-STAT4 signaling activity; population-average risk for rheumatoid arthritis and related autoimmune conditions.

TG.description: Heterozygous carrier of the G risk allele within STAT4 intron 3. Associated with moderately increased susceptibility to rheumatoid arthritis and systemic lupus erythematosus in GWAS discovery cohorts.

GG.description: Homozygous G allele; both copies carry the risk-associated STAT4 intronic variant, conferring the highest relative risk for rheumatoid arthritis at this locus in population-based studies.

description: rs7574865 is an intronic variant in STAT4 (signal transducer and activator of transcription 4) on chromosome 2q32. STAT4 is a transcription factor activated downstream of the JAK-STAT signaling pathway, particularly in response to IL-12 and IFN-α. Activated STAT4 drives differentiation of naive T cells toward the Th1 phenotype and promotes IFN-γ production, a key cytokine in autoimmune joint inflammation. The G risk allele alters STAT4 splicing or enhancer activity, increasing STAT4-driven Th1 and Th17 responses that contribute to synovial inflammation in rheumatoid arthritis.

sources.name: Remmers EF et al. (2007) - STAT4 and the risk of rheumatoid arthritis and systemic lupus erythematosus

notes: This is a risk factor, not a diagnosis. Consult a healthcare provider. The G allele at rs7574865 confers approximately 1.3–1.5-fold elevated risk for rheumatoid arthritis, with similar effect size for SLE. G allele frequency is approximately 22–23% globally and varies across ancestries. HLA-DRB1 shared epitope alleles and smoking are stronger RA risk factors; STAT4 variants contribute to polygenic risk alongside PTPN22, PADI4, and HLA loci.

CC.description: Homozygous reference allele associated with typical sclerostin expression, maintaining normal Wnt-mediated bone formation signaling.

CT.description: Heterozygous carriers may show subtle changes in sclerostin levels, modestly affecting the balance between bone formation and resorption.

TT.description: Homozygous alternate allele linked to higher sclerostin expression, which suppresses Wnt signaling and reduces osteoblast activity, increasing bone loss risk.

description: SOST encodes sclerostin, a Wnt pathway antagonist produced by osteocytes that inhibits bone formation. Elevated sclerostin levels reduce osteoblast function and bone accrual. Variants near the SOST locus influence bone mineral density and fracture risk in GWAS studies.

sources.name: Leupin et al. (2011) - SOST and bone mineral density GWAS

notes: Sclerostin inhibitor romosozumab is an approved osteoporosis treatment. This SNP informs polygenic risk but is not used for clinical decisions alone. This is a risk factor, not a diagnosis.

AA.description: Reduced SOST expression, less inhibition of bone formation, potentially higher bone density

AG.description: Moderate SOST expression, average bone formation regulation

GG.description: Standard SOST expression, normal Wnt-mediated bone formation inhibition

description: SOST encodes sclerostin, a Wnt pathway inhibitor secreted by osteocytes that suppresses bone formation. Anti-sclerostin antibodies (romosozumab) are a breakthrough osteoporosis treatment.
notes: Loss-of-function SOST mutations cause sclerosteosis (extremely dense bones). This validated sclerostin as a drug target. Romosozumab (anti-sclerostin) increases bone density more than any other osteoporosis drug.

AAAAAAAAAA.description: Homozygous 5A allele (ref/ref) associated with increased MMP3 promoter activity and elevated stromelysin levels, promoting greater matrix degradation.

AAAAAAAAAAA.description: Heterozygous carriers (5A/6A) show intermediate MMP3 expression; cartilage turnover is between the two homozygous states.

AAAAAAAAAAAA.description: Homozygous 6A allele (alt/alt) associated with reduced MMP3 transcription and slower cartilage matrix degradation under inflammatory conditions.

description: The rs3025058 5A/6A insertion-deletion polymorphism in the MMP3 promoter is one of the most studied variants affecting stromelysin-1 expression. The 5A allele drives higher transcription, while 6A reduces it, influencing cartilage integrity in osteoarthritis and post-traumatic joint disease.

sources.name: Tsezou et al. (1999) - MMP3 promoter and cartilage

notes: 5A allele is associated with higher MMP3 and possibly faster cartilage wear under load. Anti-inflammatory lifestyle reduces matrix degradation risk. This is a risk factor, not a diagnosis. This variant uses simplified nucleotide notation (AAAAA/AAAAAA) for the -1171 5A/6A poly-A insertion polymorphism in the MMP-3 promoter. Note: This insertion/deletion polymorphism may not produce phenotype results from standard DTC genotyping chips, which typically report indels in I/D format rather than nucleotide sequences.

AA.description: Strongly elevated TNF-alpha, significantly increased rheumatoid arthritis and autoimmune disease risk

AG.description: Increased TNF-alpha transcription, moderately elevated inflammatory and autoimmune disease risk

GG.description: Standard TNF-alpha expression, population-average inflammatory response

description: TNF-308G>A promoter variant increases TNF-alpha expression, a master pro-inflammatory cytokine. Associated with rheumatoid arthritis, inflammatory bowel disease, and other autoimmune conditions.
notes: TNF-alpha is a therapeutic target — anti-TNF biologics (infliximab, adalimumab, etanercept) revolutionized treatment of RA and IBD. The A allele increases transcription ~2-fold.

AA.description: Homozygous reference allele associated with typical tenascin-C expression and normal extracellular matrix remodeling in tendons.

AT.description: Heterozygous carriers may have mildly altered tenascin-C function, with a modest increase in tendinopathy susceptibility under repetitive loading.

TT.description: Homozygous alternate allele linked to reduced tenascin-C stability in tendon ECM, increasing susceptibility to chronic tendinopathy.

description: TNC encodes tenascin-C, an extracellular matrix glycoprotein highly expressed in tendons during mechanical loading and repair. Variants in TNC affect matrix resilience and have been associated with Achilles and patellar tendinopathy risk in athletes.

sources.name: September et al. (2009) - TNC and tendinopathy

notes: Risk is amplified by high-volume repetitive loading sports. Eccentric strengthening programs reduce tendinopathy risk independent of genotype. This is a risk factor, not a diagnosis.

TT.description: Homozygous reference allele associated with typical deiodinase 2 activity, standard T4-to-T3 conversion, and normal bone turnover rate.

TC.description: Heterozygous carriers may experience modest alterations in peripheral T3 levels and mild effects on osteoblast and osteoclast balance.

CC.description: Homozygous alternate allele linked to reduced DIO2 efficiency, impaired T3 bioavailability in bone tissue, and potentially altered bone mineral density.

description: DIO2 encodes type 2 iodothyronine deiodinase, which converts thyroxine (T4) to the active triiodothyronine (T3) locally in tissues including bone. Variants affecting DIO2 activity alter thyroid hormone signaling in osteoblasts and influence bone remodeling rates.

sources.name: Peeters et al. (2005) - DIO2 and bone metabolism

notes: Effect on bone most relevant in context of subclinical thyroid dysfunction. Thyroid function tests are more diagnostic than this SNP alone. This is a risk factor, not a diagnosis.

trait: Type I Collagen Alpha-2 / Bone Strength

CC.description: Homozygous reference allele associated with typical type I collagen cross-linking and normal bone strength.

CT.description: Heterozygous carriers may show subtle changes in collagen fibril architecture and modestly reduced fracture resistance.

TT.description: Homozygous alternate allele linked to altered COL1A2 transcription, potentially compromising type I collagen integrity and bone toughness.

description: COL1A2 encodes the alpha-2 chain of type I collagen, the primary structural protein of bone and connective tissue. Variants affecting its promoter region can alter expression and impact bone mineral density and fracture risk.

sources.name: Mann et al. (1997) - COL1A2 and bone density

notes: Collagen structure variants affect both bone and connective tissue. Effect is additive with COL1A1 variants. This is a risk factor, not a diagnosis.

CC.description: Homozygous reference allele associated with typical type II collagen production and normal articular cartilage matrix composition.

CT.description: Heterozygous carriers may have modest reductions in COL2A1 expression, slightly increasing susceptibility to cartilage degradation.

TT.description: Homozygous alternate allele linked to reduced type II collagen in articular cartilage and elevated osteoarthritis susceptibility.

description: COL2A1 encodes type II collagen, the principal structural protein in articular cartilage and intervertebral discs. Variants in this gene influence cartilage matrix quality and are associated with early-onset osteoarthritis and chondrodysplasias.

sources.name: Loughlin et al. (2004) - COL2A1 and osteoarthritis

notes: Associated with osteoarthritis and intervertebral disc degeneration. Diet and physical activity modulate cartilage health independently. This is a risk factor, not a diagnosis.

AA.description: VDR BsmI BB genotype, associated with slightly lower bone mineral density

AG.description: VDR BsmI Bb genotype, average bone mineral density

GG.description: VDR BsmI bb genotype, associated with slightly higher bone mineral density

description: VDR BsmI polymorphism in intron 8 affects VDR mRNA stability and expression levels. One of the classic VDR variants studied for osteoporosis risk.
notes: BsmI is in the 3' region of VDR and affects mRNA stability through LD with a poly-A microsatellite. In LD with TaqI (rs731236) and ApaI variants. Effect on BMD is modest and population-dependent.

AA.description: Enhanced Cdx2 binding, increased intestinal VDR expression and calcium absorption

AG.description: Moderate Cdx2 binding, intermediate vitamin D receptor levels

GG.description: Reduced Cdx2 transcription factor binding, lower intestinal VDR and calcium absorption

description: VDR Cdx2 polymorphism affects binding of the intestinal transcription factor Cdx2 to the VDR promoter, directly influencing intestinal calcium absorption and bone health.
notes: The A allele creates a stronger Cdx2 binding site, increasing VDR transcription in intestinal cells. This is functionally distinct from the FokI, BsmI, and TaqI VDR polymorphisms.

CC.description: Altered VDR mRNA stability, slightly reduced vitamin D receptor expression and calcium absorption

CT.description: Intermediate VDR expression, average calcium metabolism

TT.description: Higher VDR mRNA stability, better calcium absorption and bone mineral density

description: VDR TaqI polymorphism in exon 9 is a synonymous variant affecting mRNA stability through 3' UTR haplotype structure. Part of the classic VDR polymorphism panel for bone health.
notes: TaqI is in strong LD with BsmI (rs1544410) and poly-A length variation. Despite being synonymous, affects VDR expression via mRNA stability. Effect on BMD is modest and population-dependent.

CC.description: Standard VDR protein length, normal vitamin D signaling and bone metabolism

CT.description: Heterozygous VDR FokI, intermediate vitamin D receptor activity

TT.description: Extended VDR protein with altered activity, may affect bone mineral density and calcium absorption

description: VDR FokI polymorphism affects the start codon of the vitamin D receptor, producing proteins of different lengths with varying transcriptional activity. Influences bone mineral density and calcium metabolism.
notes: FokI polymorphism is unique among VDR variants as it actually changes the protein structure (3 amino acids shorter). The shorter protein (f allele, C) has slightly higher transcriptional activity.

CC.description: Normal LRP5/Wnt signaling, standard bone mineral density regulation

CT.description: Mildly impaired Wnt signaling, slightly lower bone mineral density

TT.description: Impaired LRP5/Wnt signaling, lower bone mineral density and increased fracture risk

description: LRP5 A1330V variant impairs the Wnt signaling co-receptor, reducing osteoblast activity and bone formation. A key genetic determinant of bone mineral density.
notes: LRP5 is a Wnt pathway co-receptor essential for bone formation. Gain-of-function mutations cause high bone mass, while loss-of-function causes osteoporosis-pseudoglioma syndrome. Val1330 reduces Wnt signaling ~15%.

GG.description: Homozygous reference; standard GluA1 subunit trafficking supports normal AMPA receptor-mediated LTP and synaptic strengthening.

GA.description: Heterozygous; partial alteration in GluA1 expression or trafficking may modestly affect AMPA receptor density and synaptic scaling.

AA.description: Homozygous alternate; variant associated with altered GluA1-containing receptor trafficking or expression, potentially affecting Hebbian plasticity mechanisms.

description: GRIA1 encodes the GluA1 (GluR-A) subunit of AMPA-type ionotropic glutamate receptors, which mediate fast excitatory synaptic transmission and synaptic plasticity. GluA1 trafficking to and from the postsynaptic density is the primary molecular mechanism underlying long-term potentiation and depression. Variants at rs1805247 have been studied in relation to learning and memory-related phenotypes.

sources.name: Shibata et al. (2008) - Association study of the GRIA1 gene variants with attention deficit hyperactivity disorder

notes: GRIA1 variants are studied in ADHD, working memory, and addiction research. This is a risk factor, not a diagnosis. Consult a healthcare provider.

TT.description: Homozygous reference T allele; standard ANK3 expression and ankyrin-G scaffolding at the axon initial segment. Normal voltage-gated sodium and potassium channel anchoring with typical neuronal excitability.

TC.description: Heterozygous TC; one C allele at this ANK3 intronic locus. Ankyrin-G function and axon initial segment ion channel organization may be modestly altered, with intermediate effects on neuronal excitability parameters.

CC.description: Homozygous C allele; both ANK3 alleles carry this intronic variant. May alter ankyrin-G-dependent anchoring of voltage-gated sodium and potassium channels at the neuronal axon initial segment, affecting action potential threshold and neuronal excitability.

description: ANK3 encodes ankyrin-G, a scaffolding protein critical for the organization of the axon initial segment (AIS) — the neuronal region where action potentials are initiated. Ankyrin-G anchors voltage-gated sodium channels (Nav1.1, Nav1.6), potassium channels, and cell adhesion molecules at the AIS, precisely controlling neuronal excitability and firing threshold. The rs10994359 intronic variant is located within a GWAS-identified bipolar disorder risk locus in ANK3; the nearby rs10994336 reached genome-wide significance (p=9.1×10-9) in the Ferreira et al. bipolar GWAS, and this locus represents one of the most robustly replicated genetic risk factors for bipolar disorder.

sources.name: Ferreira MA et al. (2008) - Collaborative genome-wide association analysis supports a role for ANK3 and CACNA1C in bipolar disorder

notes: ANK3 variants including rs10994359 and the nearby rs10994336 have been identified in GWAS studies as risk loci for bipolar disorder (Ferreira et al. 2008, p=9.1e-9). The population attributable risk is modest (OR ~1.15-1.3 per allele). Bipolar disorder is a complex polygenic condition influenced by hundreds of common variants, rare variants, and environmental factors; no single variant is deterministic. Genetic counseling is recommended for interpretation. This represents a statistical risk factor identified through population studies. This is a risk factor, not a diagnosis. Consult a healthcare provider before making medical decisions.

GG.description: Normal alpha-synuclein expression, population-average Parkinson's disease risk

GT.description: Modestly increased SNCA expression, slightly elevated Parkinson's disease susceptibility

TT.description: Increased alpha-synuclein expression, higher risk of Parkinson's disease and Lewy body pathology

description: SNCA 3' region variant affects alpha-synuclein expression levels. Alpha-synuclein aggregation into Lewy bodies is the pathological hallmark of Parkinson's disease.
notes: SNCA was the first Parkinson's disease gene identified. Common variants in the 3' region modestly increase risk by elevating expression. SNCA duplications/triplications cause familial Parkinson's with gene-dosage effect. This is a risk factor, not a diagnosis. Consult a healthcare provider before making medical decisions.

CC.phenotype: No e2 Allele at This Position
CC.description: Standard APOE at this position, baseline risk (must consider rs429358 for full haplotype)

CT.description: One copy of protective e2 allele, ~40% reduced Alzheimer's risk

TT.description: Two copies of protective e2 allele, significantly reduced Alzheimer's risk

description: APOE rs7412 defines the e2 allele (with rs429358). APOE e2 is protective against Alzheimer's disease, associated with more efficient amyloid-beta clearance.
notes: APOE e2 (rs429358-T + rs7412-T) reduces Alzheimer's risk by ~40-50% per allele. However, e2/e2 homozygotes have increased risk of type III hyperlipoproteinemia. ~7% of population carries at least one e2. This is a risk factor, not a diagnosis. Consult a healthcare provider before making medical decisions.

CC.description: Homozygous reference; standard ABCB1-encoded P-glycoprotein activity maintains efficient efflux of substrates across the blood-brain barrier.

CT.description: Heterozygous; partial reduction in P-gp efflux capacity may moderately increase CNS exposure to P-gp substrate drugs and endogenous compounds.

TT.description: Homozygous alternate; lower P-glycoprotein efficiency at the blood-brain barrier, potentially increasing CNS penetration of certain substrates.

description: ABCB1 encodes P-glycoprotein (P-gp), an ATP-binding cassette transporter highly expressed at the blood-brain barrier endothelium. P-gp actively pumps xenobiotics and endogenous compounds out of the CNS, limiting their brain exposure. Variants in ABCB1 are associated with altered drug pharmacokinetics and BBB permeability characteristics.

sources.name: Uhr et al. (2004) - Polymorphisms in the drug transporter gene ABCB1 predict antidepressant treatment response in depression

notes: ABCB1 variants may influence CNS drug response; relevant to pharmacogenomics. This is a risk factor, not a diagnosis. Consult a healthcare provider.

CC.description: Efficient FADS2 conversion, adequate brain DHA for cognitive development

CG.description: Moderate FADS2 activity, intermediate DHA availability for brain development

GG.description: Reduced FADS2 activity, lower endogenous DHA production, may benefit from direct DHA supplementation

description: FADS2 variant affecting DHA (docosahexaenoic acid) synthesis in the brain. DHA is critical for neuronal membrane fluidity, synaptogenesis, and cognitive development.
notes: DHA comprises ~40% of polyunsaturated fatty acids in the brain. This FADS2 variant interacts with breastfeeding status — the IQ benefit of breastfeeding may be modulated by infant FADS2 genotype. This is a risk factor, not a diagnosis. Consult a healthcare provider before making medical decisions.

AA.description: Homozygous reference A allele; standard intronic variant context within BDNF. BDNF transcription from multiple promoters is at baseline, supporting standard synaptic plasticity and neurotrophin availability for hippocampal and cortical circuits.

AG.description: Heterozygous AG; one copy of the G allele at this intronic position. The G allele is associated with modestly altered BDNF expression patterns in brain tissue in several population studies, particularly in East Asian cohorts where the G allele frequency reaches 45–50%.

GG.description: Homozygous G allele; intronic position may influence BDNF alternative splicing or promoter usage. Associated with modified BDNF protein levels and studied in relation to hippocampal volume, cognitive function, and antidepressant response in multiple East Asian populations.

description: BDNF (brain-derived neurotrophic factor) is the most abundant neurotrophin in the mammalian brain, essential for hippocampal neurogenesis, long-term potentiation, synaptic plasticity, and neuronal survival signaling through TrkB receptors. rs11030104 is an intronic BDNF variant whose G allele has been associated with altered BDNF expression in brain-derived tissue, modified hippocampal volume, and differential antidepressant response in psychiatric pharmacogenomic studies. The mechanism involves potential effects on alternative splicing of BDNF transcripts driven by multiple tissue-specific promoters.

sources.name: Egan MF et al. (2004) - The BDNF val66met polymorphism affects activity-dependent secretion of BDNF and human memory and hippocampal function

notes: G allele frequency varies dramatically by ancestry: ~45–50% in East Asian populations versus ~4% in African populations, with European populations intermediate (~15–25%). This variant is in partial LD with the more studied BDNF Val66Met (rs6265) and may partly capture the same biological signal. BDNF signaling intersects with neurological and psychiatric conditions including depression, anxiety, PTSD, Alzheimer's disease, and treatment-resistant depression response. This is a risk factor, not a diagnosis. Consult a healthcare provider before making medical decisions.

GG.description: Higher COMT enzyme activity, faster dopamine breakdown (Val/Val). Reduced prefrontal dopamine levels; associated with better stress resilience under acute challenge.

GA.description: Intermediate dopamine metabolism (Val/Met), balanced prefrontal dopamine availability and cognitive-emotional profile.

AA.description: Lower COMT enzyme activity, slower dopamine breakdown (Met/Met). Higher prefrontal dopamine levels; associated with better working memory under low-stress conditions.

description: COMT Val158Met variant affecting dopamine metabolism in prefrontal cortex. The Val allele produces higher enzyme activity (faster breakdown) while the Met allele produces lower enzyme activity (slower breakdown), resulting in different prefrontal dopamine levels with context-dependent cognitive and emotional effects.

sources.name: Lachman HM et al. (1996) - Human catechol-O-methyltransferase pharmacogenetics: description of a functional polymorphism and its potential application to neuropsychiatric disorders

notes: COMT Val158Met polymorphism — one of the most studied functional variants in psychiatric genetics. Val allele ('Warrior') has higher enzyme activity and lower prefrontal dopamine; Met allele ('Worrier') has lower activity and higher dopamine. Effects on cognition are context-dependent and modest. This is a risk factor, not a diagnosis. Consult a healthcare provider before making medical decisions.

GG.description: Homozygous reference; higher COMT enzyme activity leads to faster degradation of dopamine and norepinephrine in the prefrontal cortex.

GA.description: Heterozygous; intermediate catecholamine degradation rate, balancing prefrontal dopamine availability between high and low extremes.

AA.description: Homozygous alternate; reduced COMT activity preserves dopamine longer in prefrontal synapses, potentially improving working memory under low-stress conditions.

description: COMT encodes catechol-O-methyltransferase, an enzyme that methylates and inactivates catecholamine neurotransmitters including dopamine, epinephrine, and norepinephrine. In the prefrontal cortex, COMT activity is a primary determinant of extracellular dopamine levels. The rs165599 variant in the 3' UTR region influences COMT mRNA stability and expression.

sources.name: Shifman S et al. (2004) - A highly significant association between a COMT haplotype and schizophrenia

notes: COMT rs165599 is in the 3' UTR and affects expression; distinct from the Val158Met (rs4680) coding variant. This is a risk factor, not a diagnosis. Consult a healthcare provider.

CC.description: Two copies of e4 allele, 8-15x increased Alzheimer's risk, earlier onset of cognitive decline

CT.description: One copy of e4 allele, 3-4x increased Alzheimer's risk, potential earlier cognitive decline

TT.phenotype: No e4 Allele at This Position
TT.description: No e4 allele from this SNP, baseline Alzheimer's risk (must consider rs7412 for full APOE haplotype)

description: APOE rs429358 defines the e4 allele (with rs7412). APOE e4 is the strongest common genetic risk factor for late-onset Alzheimer's disease, affecting amyloid-beta clearance in the brain.
notes: APOE haplotype requires both rs429358 and rs7412: e2=T/T, e3=T/C, e4=C/C at these two loci. e4 homozygotes have ~50% Alzheimer's risk by age 85. Most impactful common genetic variant for dementia. This is a risk factor, not a diagnosis. Consult a healthcare provider before making medical decisions.

AA.description: Increased FKBP5 expression, prolonged cortisol exposure, impaired HPA axis negative feedback

AG.description: Moderate FKBP5 effect on glucocorticoid receptor feedback regulation

GG.description: Standard FKBP5 levels, normal HPA axis negative feedback

description: FKBP5 variant modifying glucocorticoid receptor sensitivity and HPA axis cortisol feedback. FKBP5 (FK506 binding protein 5) is a co-chaperone of the glucocorticoid receptor that regulates its nuclear translocation and sensitivity to cortisol, influencing the efficiency of HPA axis negative feedback after stress.
notes: FKBP5 risk alleles create a gene-environment feedback loop: childhood trauma demethylates FKBP5, increasing expression, impairing cortisol feedback, and sensitizing to future stress. Epigenetic mechanism confirmed. This is a risk factor, not a diagnosis. Consult a healthcare provider before making medical decisions.

AA.description: Homozygous reference; standard DISC1 scaffolding function supporting normal synaptic signaling and neuronal migration.

AG.description: Heterozygous; one altered copy may modestly affect DISC1 protein interactions with NDE1 and PDE4B in neurons.

GG.description: Homozygous alternate; both copies carry the variant, potentially affecting DISC1 scaffolding at the centrosome and postsynaptic density.

description: DISC1 (Disrupted in Schizophrenia 1) encodes a scaffold protein involved in neurodevelopment, synaptic signaling, and neuronal migration. It interacts with PDE4B and NDE1 to regulate cAMP signaling and microtubule organization. Variants at rs12576775 have been associated with altered neurodevelopmental trajectories.

sources.name: Blackwood et al. (2001) - Schizophrenia and affective disorders—cosegregation with a translocation at chromosome 1q42 that directly disrupts brain-expressed genes: clinical and P300 findings in a family

notes: DISC1 variants are associated with neurodevelopmental conditions in research contexts. This is a risk factor, not a diagnosis. Consult a healthcare provider.

TT.description: Homozygous reference T allele; standard DBH promoter activity drives higher enzyme expression. Dopamine is efficiently converted to norepinephrine in sympathetic neurons and adrenal medulla, yielding high DBH activity and elevated plasma norepinephrine-to-dopamine ratio.

TC.description: Heterozygous TC; one C allele reduces DBH promoter activity. Intermediate plasma DBH enzyme levels and intermediate norepinephrine synthesis. Partial attenuation of sympathetic noradrenergic tone relative to TT.

CC.description: Homozygous C allele; substantially reduced DBH promoter activity, resulting in very low plasma DBH activity (~3–6-fold lower than TT). Reduced norepinephrine synthesis from dopamine in peripheral sympathetic neurons, associated with orthostatic hypotension and altered catecholamine balance.

description: DBH (dopamine beta-hydroxylase) is the enzyme that catalyzes the hydroxylation of dopamine to norepinephrine in sympathetic neurons and the adrenal medulla, and is also secreted into the circulation where plasma DBH activity is measurable. The rs1611115 T>C variant is located approximately 2 kb upstream of the DBH coding sequence and substantially modulates transcriptional activity of the DBH promoter. The C allele is the most powerful known genetic determinant of plasma DBH activity, accounting for approximately 35–50% of the population variance in circulating DBH levels and associated catecholamine profiles.

sources.name: Zabetian CP et al. (1999) - A quantitative-trait locus for variation in dopamine beta-hydroxylase activity on chromosome 9q34

notes: C allele frequency is approximately 20–30% in European populations. Low DBH activity (CC genotype) is associated with orthostatic hypotension type 1 (dopamine beta-hydroxylase deficiency), attention-deficit traits, and altered response to catecholaminergic drugs including cocaine sensitization in animal models and norepinephrine reuptake inhibitors in clinical settings. The rs1611115 locus has been studied in ADHD, schizophrenia, and substance use disorder. This is a risk factor, not a diagnosis. Consult a healthcare provider before making medical decisions.
