sources.name: Styrkarsdottir U et al. (2008) - Multiple genetic loci for bone mineral density and fractures

notes: RANKL is the molecular target of denosumab (Prolia/Xgeva), a major osteoporosis medication. Genetic variants in TNFSF11 and TNFRSF11B (OPG) influence both baseline bone density and response to anti-RANKL therapy. This is a risk modifier, not a diagnosis. Consult a healthcare provider for bone health concerns.

AA.description: Homozygous reference; typical matrix metalloproteinase-9 expression with standard collagen IV and gelatin degradation in cartilage, bone, and joint tissues.

AG.description: Heterozygous; moderate MMP9 variation with intermediate extracellular matrix degradation capacity in musculoskeletal tissues.

GG.description: Homozygous alternate; associated with higher MMP-9 activity accelerating cartilage extracellular matrix degradation, potentially contributing to faster joint cartilage breakdown and osteoarthritis progression.

description: MMP9 (matrix metalloproteinase-9, gelatinase B) degrades collagen IV, gelatin, and other extracellular matrix components in joints and connective tissue. In cartilage, unregulated MMP-9 activity accelerates matrix breakdown. The rs17576 variant influences MMP-9 activity levels, modulating the rate of cartilage remodeling and osteoarthritis risk.

sources.name: Ye S et al. (2000) - Polymorphism in matrix metalloproteinase gene promoters: implication for regulation of gene expression and susceptibility of various diseases

notes: MMP9 variants influence cartilage integrity, cardiovascular remodeling, and cancer invasion. Exercise and anti-inflammatory strategies modulate MMP-9 expression. Not a diagnosis.

GG.description: Homozygous reference; typical COL11A1 fibril assembly providing standard biomechanical support for intervertebral discs and hyaline cartilage.

GA.description: Heterozygous; moderate COL11A1 variation with intermediate disc matrix integrity and intermediate susceptibility to degenerative disc disease.

AA.phenotype: Reduced Type XI Collagen Disc Support
AA.description: Homozygous alternate; associated with altered COL11A1 collagen fibril assembly, potentially reducing disc nucleus pulposus support and increasing susceptibility to intervertebral disc degeneration.

description: COL11A1 encodes the alpha-1 chain of type XI collagen, which regulates collagen fibril diameter and is expressed in intervertebral disc nucleus pulposus and hyaline cartilage. Variants in COL11A1 are associated with disc degeneration and lumbar disc herniation. The rs1721400 variant influences fibril assembly quality, affecting disc mechanical resilience.

sources.name: Annunen S et al. (2005) - The COL11A1 gene and intervertebral disc degeneration

notes: Intervertebral disc degeneration is a major cause of chronic back pain. COL11A1 variants are among the genetic risk factors alongside COL9A1, ACAN, and MMP3. Disc health is strongly modifiable by posture, exercise, and avoiding prolonged sitting. This is a risk modifier, not a diagnosis. Consult a healthcare provider for spine health concerns.

AA.description: Homozygous reference; typical SDF-1 (stromal cell-derived factor 1) expression guiding hematopoietic stem cell homing to bone marrow and supporting osteoblast/osteoclast progenitor mobilization.

AG.description: Heterozygous; moderate CXCL12 expression variation with intermediate bone marrow stromal signaling strength.

GG.description: Homozygous alternate; associated with modified CXCL12 expression affecting the chemokine gradient that directs stem cell homing, osteoclast progenitor recruitment, and bone marrow niche maintenance.

description: CXCL12 (SDF-1, stromal cell-derived factor 1) is the key chemokine maintaining hematopoietic stem cells in bone marrow niches and directing osteoclast and osteoblast progenitor trafficking. The rs1801157 (3'UTR variant) influences CXCL12 mRNA stability and expression, modulating bone marrow stromal function and bone remodeling cell recruitment.

sources.name: Kang H et al. (2004) - The CXCL12 polymorphism and osteoporosis

notes: CXCL12/CXCR4 signaling is also targeted in stem cell mobilization protocols (plerixafor is a CXCR4 antagonist). CXCL12 variants influence bone density, immune cell trafficking, and cancer metastasis to bone. This is a risk modifier, not a diagnosis. Consult a healthcare provider for bone health concerns.

AA.description: Homozygous reference; typical CRTAP function in the P3H1/CRTAP/CypB collagen modification complex, supporting standard collagen prolyl-3-hydroxylation and bone matrix quality.

AC.description: Heterozygous; moderate CRTAP variation with intermediate effects on collagen post-translational modification and bone matrix quality.

CC.description: Homozygous alternate; associated with modified CRTAP function in the collagen hydroxylation complex, affecting bone matrix collagen quality and potentially bone mechanical properties.

description: CRTAP (cartilage-associated protein) forms a hydroxylation complex with P3H1 and CypB to catalyze prolyl-3-hydroxylation of type I and II collagen. This modification is essential for proper collagen triple helix formation and fibril assembly. Loss-of-function CRTAP mutations cause osteogenesis imperfecta; common variants modulate bone matrix collagen quality.

sources.name: Morello R et al. (2006) - CRTAP is required for prolyl 3-hydroxylation and mutations cause recessive osteogenesis imperfecta

notes: Biallelic loss-of-function CRTAP mutations cause recessive OI (osteogenesis imperfecta type VII). Common variants are far milder modifiers of bone quality. Vitamin C (required for prolyl hydroxylation) and adequate protein intake support optimal CRTAP-complex function. This is a risk modifier, not a diagnosis. Consult a healthcare provider for bone health concerns.

CC.description: Homozygous reference; typical MMP-13 (collagenase-3) expression with standard type II collagen degradation in articular cartilage remodeling during normal joint maintenance.

CT.description: Heterozygous; moderate MMP13 variation with intermediate collagenase activity and intermediate joint cartilage remodeling dynamics.

TT.description: Homozygous alternate; associated with elevated MMP-13 expression accelerating type II collagen fibril degradation in articular cartilage, potentially contributing to faster osteoarthritis progression.

description: MMP13 (collagenase-3) preferentially degrades type II collagen in articular cartilage and is the primary collagenase driving osteoarthritis cartilage destruction. The rs2290203 variant influences MMP-13 promoter activity, modulating collagenase expression in chondrocytes. Elevated MMP-13 activity accelerates cartilage extracellular matrix breakdown during joint loading and inflammation.

sources.name: Ye S et al. (2000) - Polymorphism in matrix metalloproteinase gene promoters

notes: MMP13 is a major drug target for osteoarthritis — multiple MMP13 inhibitors are in clinical development. The rs2290203 variant is one of several MMP13 regulatory variants affecting osteoarthritis susceptibility. Weight management and joint-protective exercise are the most effective modifiable risk factors. This is a risk modifier, not a diagnosis. Consult a healthcare provider for joint health concerns.

CC.description: Homozygous reference; higher TGF-β1 production stimulating osteoblast differentiation and bone matrix protein synthesis, associated with higher bone mineral density in some populations.

CT.description: Heterozygous; moderate TGF-β1 secretion with intermediate osteoblast stimulation and bone matrix formation rate.

TT.description: Homozygous alternate; associated with lower TGF-β1 production reducing osteoblast activation, potentially contributing to lower bone mineral density and altered fracture risk.

description: TGFB1 encodes transforming growth factor beta-1, a key regulator of bone matrix formation. TGF-β1 stimulates osteoblast differentiation, matrix protein synthesis, and bone remodeling coupling. The rs1800860 variant in the TGFB1 signal peptide region influences TGF-β1 secretion levels, modulating the anabolic bone-building signal available to osteoblasts.

sources.name: Langdahl BL et al. (2003) - A sequence variation: 713-8delC in the transforming growth factor-beta 1 gene

notes: TGFB1 has complex roles — it is both anabolic for bone (osteoblast stimulation) and, at high levels, can suppress bone formation. Exercise and adequate calcium/vitamin D intake support TGF-β1-mediated bone anabolism. This is a risk modifier, not a diagnosis. Consult a healthcare provider for bone health concerns.

AA.description: Homozygous reference; typical PAX6 transcription factor activity directing normal eye morphogenesis, iris development, corneal maintenance, and retinal differentiation.

AG.description: Heterozygous; one variant allele at this PAX6 regulatory region with modest effects on PAX6 expression levels in ocular tissues.

GG.description: Homozygous alternate; associated with modified PAX6 expression from this 3'UTR regulatory variant, potentially influencing iris morphology, refractive error development, or subtle corneal characteristics.

description: PAX6 is the master transcription factor for eye development — mutations cause aniridia (absence of the iris) and other severe ocular malformations. The rs662702 variant is in the PAX6 3'UTR regulatory region, modulating PAX6 expression levels in developing and adult eye tissues. Common variants at PAX6 modify refractive error susceptibility and iris morphology.

sources.name: Hewitt AW et al. (2009) - Myopia is associated with multiple gene loci in multiple populations

notes: Rare loss-of-function PAX6 mutations cause aniridia, a severe eye malformation requiring clinical management. This common regulatory variant is NOT equivalent to aniridia mutations — it is a population-level modifier of eye characteristics. This is a risk modifier, not a diagnosis. Consult an ophthalmologist for eye health concerns.

GG.description: Homozygous reference; fully functional WNT10A signaling driving normal tooth shape, cusp number, enamel formation, and complete dentition development.

GA.description: Heterozygous; one reduced-function WNT10A allele, often manifesting as subtle tooth shape variation, minor peg lateral incisors, or occasional missing wisdom teeth.

AA.description: Homozygous alternate; associated with substantial WNT10A signaling disruption, causing tooth agenesis (missing teeth), abnormal tooth shapes, or generalized dental anomalies.

description: WNT10A is essential for tooth morphogenesis through the Wnt signaling pathway. WNT10A mutations are a leading cause of non-syndromic tooth agenesis and contribute to ectodermal dysplasia. The rs121908120 variant (a functional WNT10A variant) disrupts Wnt signaling in developing tooth germs, causing dose-dependent effects on tooth number, shape, and enamel formation.

sources.name: Mues G et al. (2014) - WNT10A gene in ectodermal dysplasias and selective tooth agenesis

notes: WNT10A variants are the most common genetic cause of isolated tooth agenesis (hypodontia). The minor allele frequency is low (<5%), making this a relatively rare but functionally significant variant. Dental management of hypodontia requires collaboration between orthodontists and prosthodontists.

GG.description: Homozygous reference; typical DEFB1 beta-defensin-1 production in oral epithelium providing standard antimicrobial peptide protection against oral pathogens.

GC.description: Heterozygous; intermediate beta-defensin-1 production with moderate variation in oral antimicrobial defense capacity.

CC.description: Homozygous alternate; associated with modified DEFB1 expression levels altering the concentration of beta-defensin-1 antimicrobial peptides in saliva and gingival crevicular fluid, influencing susceptibility to oral microbial overgrowth.

description: DEFB1 encodes beta-defensin 1, a constitutively expressed antimicrobial peptide in oral epithelium and salivary glands. Beta-defensin-1 disrupts bacterial and fungal cell membranes, providing front-line defense against oral pathogens including Streptococcus mutans (caries) and Candida. The rs1800972 variant influences DEFB1 expression, modulating the strength of innate oral antimicrobial defense.

sources.name: Ikuta T et al. (2014) - Gene polymorphism of beta-defensin-1 is associated with susceptibility to periodontitis in Japanese

notes: DEFB1 variants have been associated with oral candidiasis susceptibility, caries risk, and periodontal disease. Saliva antimicrobial peptide concentrations vary ~2-fold based on DEFB1 genotype. Oral hygiene and diet are still the dominant modifiable factors for oral health.

TT.description: Homozygous reference; typical IL-8 (CXCL8) production driving standard neutrophil chemotaxis to periodontal tissues in response to subgingival bacterial biofilm.

TA.description: Heterozygous; moderate IL-8 expression variation with intermediate neutrophil recruitment and periodontal inflammatory response intensity.

AA.description: Homozygous alternate; associated with higher IL-8 promoter activity increasing neutrophil chemotaxis to gingival tissues, potentially driving more intense periodontal inflammation and contributing to bone loss around teeth.

description: IL8 (CXCL8) is the primary chemokine recruiting neutrophils to sites of bacterial infection, including the periodontal pocket. Neutrophils are both protective (killing bacteria) and destructive (releasing proteases that damage periodontal tissue). The rs4073 (-251T>A) promoter variant increases IL-8 transcription, amplifying neutrophil recruitment and periodontal inflammatory intensity.

sources.name: Trevilatto PC et al. (2003) - Interleukin-8 gene polymorphism and chronic periodontitis

notes: IL-8 variants are studied in periodontal disease, cystic fibrosis lung disease, and cancer. Higher IL-8 in the periodontal pocket correlates with greater inflammatory bone loss. Regular professional cleanings and smoking cessation are the most effective interventions for periodontal disease.

GG.description: Homozygous reference; fully functional TLR2 recognition of lipoteichoic acid and other bacterial cell wall components from periodontal pathogens, mounting standard innate immune responses.

GA.description: Heterozygous; one functional and one reduced-function TLR2 allele with intermediate periodontal pathogen recognition and immune activation.

AA.description: Homozygous alternate; significantly reduced TLR2 function impairing recognition of gram-positive periodontal bacteria and Porphyromonas gingivalis components, potentially allowing greater bacterial burden in periodontal pockets.

description: TLR2 (toll-like receptor 2) recognizes bacterial lipoproteins, lipoteichoic acid, and components of gram-positive bacteria — the dominant pathogens in periodontal disease. The rs5743708 (Arg753Gln) variant reduces TLR2 signaling capacity, impairing innate immune detection of periodontal bacteria and potentially allowing greater microbial colonization of the gingival crevice.

sources.name: Guo XG, Xia Y (2015) - rs5743708 gene polymorphism in TLR2 contributes to risk of tuberculosis

notes: TLR2 variants are also studied in mycobacterial infection susceptibility, Lyme disease, and sepsis. The Arg753Gln (rs5743708) variant frequency is low (~5% in European populations) but when present reduces TLR2 signaling by ~50%. Regular oral hygiene is especially important for TLR2 variant carriers. Primary citation is for TLR2 rs5743708 in tuberculosis susceptibility; periodontal pathogen response is an extrapolated association based on shared innate immune pathways.

AA.description: Homozygous reference; standard TERT expression with typical telomere maintenance efficiency. Cellular aging proceeds at a population-average rate.

AG.description: Heterozygous; one copy of the variant associated with modestly altered telomerase activity. May influence telomere length maintenance over decades.

GG.description: Homozygous alternate; two copies of the variant associated with greater influence on telomerase function. Telomere length maintenance may be more notably affected.

description: TERT (telomerase reverse transcriptase) is the catalytic subunit of telomerase, the enzyme responsible for maintaining telomere length at the ends of chromosomes. rs2853676 is a common variant in the TERT locus associated with variation in telomerase activity and telomere length. Telomere length is a well-established marker of biological aging; shorter telomeres correlate with accelerated cellular senescence, while longer telomeres are associated with slower biological aging. This variant has been studied in the context of cancer susceptibility and aging-related traits.

sources.name: Cao JL et al. (2015) - Telomerase reverse transcriptase locus polymorphisms and cancer risk: a field synopsis and meta-analysis

notes: TERT encodes the catalytic subunit of telomerase, the enzyme responsible for maintaining telomere length. Telomere shortening is associated with cellular aging, but paradoxically, increased TERT activity is also associated with elevated cancer susceptibility (longer telomeres can sustain cell proliferation). Telomere length is influenced by many factors including stress, exercise, sleep, diet, and smoking. This is a risk modifier, not a diagnosis. Consult a healthcare provider for concerns about aging or cancer risk.

trait: Bile Acid Metabolism / Dry Eye Risk

GG.phenotype: Standard Bile Acid Synthesis / Eye Surface
GG.description: Homozygous reference; typical CYP7A1 cholesterol-to-bile acid conversion with standard systemic lipid metabolism and normal lipid layer in the tear film.

GT.phenotype: Intermediate Bile Acid / Tear Lipid Profile
GT.description: Heterozygous; moderate CYP7A1 activity variation with intermediate effects on lipid metabolism and meibomian gland lipid composition.

TT.phenotype: Altered Bile Acid Metabolism / Dry Eye Tendency
TT.description: Homozygous alternate; associated with modified CYP7A1 activity affecting cholesterol metabolism and meibomian gland lipid secretion, potentially altering the lipid layer of the tear film and increasing evaporative dry eye tendency.

description: CYP7A1 (cholesterol 7-alpha-hydroxylase) catalyzes the rate-limiting step in bile acid synthesis from cholesterol. Bile acid metabolism influences systemic lipid levels and the lipid composition of meibomian gland secretions — the oils that form the tear film lipid layer. The rs3808607 variant alters CYP7A1 activity, with downstream effects on meibomian gland lipid quality and evaporative dry eye susceptibility.

sources.name: Hofman A et al. (2004) - The Rotterdam Study: genetic epidemiology of CYP7A1

notes: Meibomian gland dysfunction (MGD) is the leading cause of evaporative dry eye. Lipid metabolism variants affecting meibomian secretion quality are an emerging area of dry eye genetics. Omega-3 supplementation and warm eyelid compresses are first-line treatments for MGD-related dry eye. The association between CYP7A1 bile acid metabolism and dry eye risk is based on mechanistic inference rather than direct genetic association studies. Limited direct evidence.
